Detailed Description

Represents a graph structure used for bucket-based optimization in a solver.

The BucketGraph class provides various functionalities for managing and optimizing a graph structure using buckets. It includes methods for initialization, configuration printing, node visitation checks, statistics printing, bucket assignment, and more.

The class also supports parallel arc generation, label management, and feasibility checks. It is designed to work with different directions (Forward and Backward) and stages of the optimization process.

Note
This class relies on several preprocessor directives (e.g., RIH, RCC, SRC) to conditionally enable or disable certain features.
Template Parameters
DirectionThe direction of the graph (Forward or Backward).
StageThe stage of the optimization process.
FullIndicates whether the full labeling algorithm should be used.
ArcTypeThe type of arc (Bucket or regular).
MutabilityIndicates whether the label is mutable or immutable.

BucketGraph::BucketGraph ( const std::vector< VRPNode > & nodes,
int horizon,
int bucket_interval,
int capacity,
int capacity_interval )

Constructs a BucketGraph object.

This constructor initializes a BucketGraph with the given nodes, time horizon, and bucket interval. It sets up the forward and backward buckets, initializes the dual values for the CVRP separation, and defines the intervals and resource limits.

◆ assign_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
auto & BucketGraph::assign_buckets ( auto & FW,
auto & BW )
inlineconstexprnoexcept

Assigns the buckets based on the specified direction.

This function returns a reference to the buckets based on the specified direction. If the direction is Forward, it returns a reference to the forward buckets. If the direction is Backward, it returns a reference to the backward buckets.

Template Parameters
DThe direction (Forward or Backward).
Parameters
FWThe forward buckets.
BWThe backward buckets.
Returns
A reference to the buckets based on the specified direction.

void BucketGraph::augment_ng_memories ( std::vector< double > & solution,
std::vector< Path > & paths,
bool aggressive,
int eta1,
int eta2,
int eta_max,
int nC )

Augments the memories in the BucketGraph.

This function takes a solution vector, a SparseModel, and several parameters to augment the memories in the BucketGraph. It identifies cycles in the SparseModel that meet certain conditions and forbids them in the BucketGraph. The function prioritizes smaller cycles and limits the number of forbidden cycles based on the given parameters.

Definition at line 391 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

393 {
394 // Pre-allocate an estimated number of cycles.
395 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> cycles;
396 cycles.reserve(paths.size() / 4);
397 ng_iteration_counter++;
398
399 // // When a cycle is encountered in the current solution:
400 // for (auto &data : ng_cycles) {
401 // if (cycleMatchesCurrentSolution(data.cycle, solution, paths)) {
402 // data.last_used_iteration = ng_iteration_counter;
403 // data.usage_count++;
404 // }
405 // }
406
407 // Detect cycles in fractional paths.
408 for (size_t pathIndex = 0; pathIndex < paths.size(); ++pathIndex) {
409 const double epsilon = 1e-3;
410 if (solution[pathIndex] <= epsilon) continue;
411 const auto &path = paths[pathIndex];
412 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<uint16_t, int> visited_clients;
413 visited_clients.reserve(path.size());
414
415 // Iterate over the nodes in the path to detect cycles.
416 for (size_t i = 0; i < path.size(); ++i) {
417 uint16_t client = path[i];
418 // Skip depot nodes.
419 if (client == 0 || client == N_SIZE - 1) continue;
420 auto it = visited_clients.find(client);
421 if (it != visited_clients.end()) {
422 // Cycle found: extract cycle from first occurrence to current
423 // index.
424 std::vector<uint16_t> cycle;
425 cycle.reserve(i - it->second + 1);
426 for (size_t j = it->second; j <= i; ++j)
427 cycle.push_back(path[j]);
428 cycles.push_back(std::move(cycle));
429 break; // Process only one cycle per path.
430 }
431 visited_clients[client] = i;
432 }
433 }
434 if (cycles.empty()) return;
435
436 // Sort cycles by increasing size (small cycles processed first).
437 pdqsort(cycles.begin(), cycles.end(),
438 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.size() < b.size(); });
439
440 int forbidden_count = 0;
441
442 // Cache neighborhood bit counts to avoid redundant __builtin_popcountll
443 // calls.
444 std::vector<int> cached_counts(N_SIZE, -1);
445 auto count_neighborhood_bits = [this, eta_max,
446 &cached_counts](uint16_t node) -> int {
447 if (cached_counts[node] >= 0) return cached_counts[node];
448 int count = 0;
449 for (const auto &segment : neighborhoods_bitmap[node]) {
450 count += __builtin_popcountll(segment);
451 if (count >= eta_max) break;
452 }
453 cached_counts[node] = count;
454 return count;
455 };
456
457 // Adapt thresholds based on the aggressive flag.
458 int effective_eta1 = eta1;
459 int effective_eta2 = eta2;
460
461 // Process each detected cycle.
462 for (const auto &cycle : cycles) {
463 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
464
465 bool can_forbid = true;
466 // Check if any node in the cycle has too many neighbor bits.
467 for (uint16_t node : cycle) {
468 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
469 can_forbid = false;
470 break;
471 }
472 }
473
474 // If the cycle qualifies (i.e. it's small enough or we haven't
475 // forbidden too many cycles yet).
476 if (can_forbid && (static_cast<int>(cycle.size()) <= effective_eta1 ||
477 forbidden_count < effective_eta2)) {
478 // ng_cycles.push_back({cycle, ng_iteration_counter, 1});
479 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
480 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
481 ++forbidden_count;
482 if (forbidden_count >= effective_eta2) break;
483 }
484 }
485 // If no cycle was added, try again without effective_eta1 restriction.
486 if (forbidden_count == 0) {
487 for (const auto &cycle : cycles) {
488 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
489
490 bool can_forbid = true;
491 // Check if any node in the cycle has too many neighbor bits.
492 for (uint16_t node : cycle) {
493 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
494 can_forbid = false;
495 break;
496 }
497 }
498
499 // If the cycle qualifies (i.e. it's small enough or we haven't
500 // forbidden too many cycles yet).
501 if (can_forbid && forbidden_count < effective_eta2) {
502 // ng_cycles.push_back({cycle, ng_iteration_counter, 1});
503 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
504 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
505 ++forbidden_count;
506 if (forbidden_count >= effective_eta2) break;
507 }
508 }
509 }
510}

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::bi_labeling_algorithm ( )

448 {
449 // === Stage-specific Initializations ===
450 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three) {
451 heuristic_fixing<S>();
452 } else if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
453 if (first_reset) {
454 reset_fixed();
455 first_reset = false;
456 }
457#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
458 if (options.bucket_fixing) {
459 // heuristic_fixing<S>();
460
461 bucket_fixing<S>();
462 }
463#endif
464 // heuristic_fixing<S>();
465 }
466
467 // Reset pools and perform common initialization.
468 reset_pool();
469 common_initialization();
470
471 // Pre-allocate c-bar vectors for forward and backward buckets.
472 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size());
473 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size());
474
475 // Run the labeling algorithms.
476 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
477 run_labeling_algorithms<S, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
478 } else {
479 forward_cbar =
480 labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, S, Full::Partial>();
481 }
482
483 // Acquire an initial best label from the forward label pool.
484 auto best_label = label_pool_fw->acquire();
485 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
486 // Compute the best label if the forward and backward labels are
487 // feasible.
488 if (check_feasibility(fw_best_label, bw_best_label)) {
489 best_label = compute_label<S>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
490 } else {
491 best_label->cost = 0.0;
492 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
493 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
494 }
495 } else {
496 best_label->cost = best_label->real_cost;
497 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
498 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
499 }
500 merged_labels.push_back(best_label);
501
502 // Declare atomics to track the best cost and non-dominated label
503 // count.
504 std::atomic<double> best_cost{0.0};
505 std::atomic<size_t> non_dominated_labels_per_bucket{0};
506
507 // === Parallel Bucket Processing ===
508 // Choose a chunk size based on hardware concurrency.
509 const size_t chunk_size =
510 fw_buckets_size / MERGE_SCHED_CONCURRENCY; // 1/4 of buckets per core
511 const size_t n_chunks = (fw_buckets_size + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size;
512
513 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
514 stdexec::just(), n_chunks,
515 [this, chunk_size, &best_cost,
516 &non_dominated_labels_per_bucket](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
517 const size_t start_bucket = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
518 const size_t end_bucket =
519 std::min(start_bucket + chunk_size,
520 static_cast<size_t>(fw_buckets_size));
521
522 // Process each bucket within the current chunk.
523 for (size_t bucket = start_bucket; bucket < end_bucket; ++bucket) {
524 // Process non-dominated labels in the current bucket.
525 const auto &bucket_labels = fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
526 for (const Label *L : bucket_labels) {
527 if (L->is_dominated) continue;
528
529 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket.fetch_add(
530 1, std::memory_order_relaxed);
531
532 // Retrieve the outgoing arcs for the current label.
533 // const auto &to_arcs =
534 // nodes[L->node_id].get_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
535 const auto &to_arcs =
536 fw_buckets[bucket]
537 .template get_bucket_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
538 for (const auto &arc : to_arcs) {
539 const int to_node = fw_buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id;
540 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three ||
541 S == Stage::Eliminate) {
542 if (is_arc_fixed(L->node_id, to_node)) continue;
543 }
544
545 // Extend the label along the current arc.
546 auto Ls =
547 Extend<Direction::Forward, S, ArcType::Bucket,
548 Mutability::Const, Full::Reverse>(L, arc);
549
550 // Process each extended label.
551 if (!Ls.empty()) {
552 for (Label *L_prime : Ls) {
553 int bucket_to_process =
554 L_prime->vertex; // mutable copy if needed
555
556 // Concatenate the label with bucket
557 // information, updating best_cost.
558 ConcatenateLabel<S, SYM>(L, bucket_to_process,
559 best_cost);
560 }
561 }
562 }
563 }
564 }
565 });
566
567 // Submit the bulk work to the merge scheduler and wait for
568 // completion.
569 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(merge_sched, bulk_sender);
570 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
571
572 // === Post-processing and Sorting ===
573 // Sort merged labels based on cost (ascending order).
574 pdqsort(merged_labels.begin(), merged_labels.end(),
575 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
576
577#ifdef SCHRODINGER
578 // If using the Schrödinger approach, process additional paths.
579 if (merged_labels.size() > N_ADD) {
580 std::vector<Path> paths;
581 const int labels_size = merged_labels.size();
582 const int end_idx = std::min(N_ADD + N_ADD, labels_size);
583 paths.reserve(end_idx - N_ADD);
584 for (int i = N_ADD; i < end_idx; ++i) {
585 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) continue;
586 paths.emplace_back(merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered,
587 merged_labels[i]->real_cost);
588 }
589 sPool.add_paths(paths);
590 sPool.iterate();
591 }
592#endif
593
594#ifdef RIH
595 // If using RIH stage modifications, submit the top labels to ILS.
596 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
597 std::vector<Label *> top_labels;
598 top_labels.reserve(N_ADD);
599 const int n_candidates =
600 std::min(N_ADD, static_cast<int>(merged_labels.size()));
601 for (int i = 0; i < n_candidates; ++i) {
602 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) continue;
603 top_labels.push_back(merged_labels[i]);
604 }
605 ils->submit_task(top_labels, nodes);
606 }
607#endif
608
609 // Store the cost of the best (first) merged label as the inner
610 // objective.
611 inner_obj = merged_labels[0]->cost;
612 return merged_labels;
613}

◆ bucket_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::bucket_fixing ( )

Fixes the bucket arcs for the specified stage.

This function performs the bucket arc fixing for the given stage. It initializes necessary variables and runs labeling algorithms to compute forward and backward reduced costs. Based on the computed gap, it performs arc elimination in both forward and backward directions and generates the necessary arcs.

Definition at line 1075 of file BucketUtils.h.

1075 {
1076 // Only execute if not already fixed.
1077 if (!fixed) {
1078 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
1079 fixed = true;
1080
1081 // Compute gap and check feasibility (if gap is negative, exit
1082 // early).
1083 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
1084 if (gap < 0) {
1085 fmt::print(
1086 "\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE CAN'T BE EXECUTED DUE TO "
1087 "GAP\033[0m\n");
1088 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
1089 return;
1090 }
1091
1092 // Initialize common structures, arc scores, warm labels, etc.
1093 common_initialization();
1094
1095 // Initialize forward and backward c_bar vectors.
1096 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(
1097 fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1098 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(
1099 bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1100
1101 // Run labeling algorithms for Stage Four with full mode.
1102 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar,
1103 backward_cbar);
1104 // Save computed c_bar values.
1105 fw_c_bar = forward_cbar;
1106 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
1107
1108 // Print info about bucket arc elimination.
1109 print_info("performing bucket arc elimination with theta = {}\n", gap);
1110
1111 // Run forward and backward sections in parallel.
1112 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1113 work, bi_sched,
1114 SECTION {
1115 // Section 1: Process forward direction.
1116 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Forward>(gap);
1117 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>();
1118 },
1119 SECTION {
1120 // Section 2: Process backward direction.
1121 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Backward>(gap);
1122 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>();
1123 });
1124
1125 // Generate arcs after elimination.
1126 // generate_arcs();
1127 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
1128
1129 just_fixed = true;
1130 return;
1131 }
1132 just_fixed = false;
1133}

◆ BucketArcElimination()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::BucketArcElimination ( double theta)

Eliminates bucket arcs based on a given threshold.

This function performs bucket arc elimination in either the forward or backward direction, depending on the template parameter D. It resets all elements in the fixed buckets to 0, prints information about the direction of elimination, and processes arcs to update the local bucket arc map and fixed buckets.

Definition at line 219 of file BucketJump.h.

219 {
220 // Select direction-specific containers.
221 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
222 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
223 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
224 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
225 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
226 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
227 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
228
229 // Reset fixed buckets bitmap.
230 fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(fixed_buckets_bitmap.size(), 0);
231 const int n_buckets = buckets_size;
232 // Compute the number of 64-bit segments for bitmaps.
233 const size_t n_segments = (buckets_size + 63) / 64;
234
235 // Define ArcMap to store arc information.
236 using ArcMap =
237 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, int>,
238 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int>,
239 arc_map_hash>;
240 ArcMap local_B_Ba_b;
241 int removed_arcs = 0;
242
243 // Helper: create an arc key from two node IDs and a bucket.
244 auto create_arc_key = [](int from, int to, int b) {
245 return std::make_pair(std::make_pair(from, to), b);
246 };
247
248 // Helper lambda to quickly reset a bitmap vector.
249 auto reset_bitmap = [&](std::vector<uint64_t> &bitmap) {
250 // Using memset since our vector is contiguous.
251 if (!bitmap.empty())
252 std::memset(bitmap.data(), 0, bitmap.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
253 };
254
255 // Lambda: Process jump arcs for bucket 'b'.
256 auto process_jump_arcs = [&](int b) {
257 const auto &jump_arcs = buckets[b].template get_jump_arcs<D>();
258 if (jump_arcs.empty()) return;
259
260 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
261 auto labels = buckets[b].get_non_dominated_labels();
262
263 for (const auto &a : jump_arcs) {
264 auto increment = a.resource_increment;
265 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket, b);
266 int b_opposite =
267 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.jump_bucket, increment);
268 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
269
270 // Process each label in the current bucket.
271 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
272 Bidi_map.insert(b);
273 // Reset Bvisited for each UpdateBucketsSet call.
274 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
275 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
276 Bvisited);
277 }
278 }
279 };
280
281 // Lambda: Process bucket arcs for bucket 'b'.
282 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int b) {
283 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
284 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) return;
285
286 auto labels = buckets[b].get_non_dominated_labels();
287 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
288
289 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
290 // Build the resource increment vector.
291 std::vector<double> increment(options.resources.size(), 0.0);
292 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
293 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
294 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
295 else
296 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
297 }
298
299 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id,
300 buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
301 int b_opposite =
302 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
303 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
304
305 // Process each label in bucket 'b'.
306 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
307 Bidi_map.insert(b);
308 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
309 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
310 Bvisited);
311 }
312
313 // Check if the opposite direction contains arcs.
314 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(arc_key);
315 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
316 auto &Bidi_map_opposite = it->second;
317 bool contains = std::any_of(
318 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].begin(),
319 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].end(), [&](int b_prime) {
320 return Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_prime) !=
321 Bidi_map_opposite.end();
322 });
323 if (!contains && Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_opposite) ==
324 Bidi_map_opposite.end()) {
325 size_t bit_pos =
326 static_cast<size_t>(a.from_bucket) * n_buckets +
327 a.to_bucket;
328 fixed_buckets_bitmap[bit_pos / 64] |=
329 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
330 ++removed_arcs;
331 }
332 }
333 }
334 };
335
336 // Process each bucket.
337 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
338 // Process node arcs.
339 const auto &node_arcs =
340 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
341 for (const auto &a : node_arcs) {
342 auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b);
343 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
344
345 if (!Phi[b].empty()) {
346 Bidi_map.insert(b);
347 // Merge neighbor bucket arcs.
348 for (const auto &b_prime : Phi[b]) {
349 auto neighbor_key =
350 std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b_prime);
351 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(neighbor_key);
352 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
353 Bidi_map.insert(it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
354 }
355 }
356 }
357 }
358
359 // Process jump arcs and bucket arcs.
360 process_jump_arcs(b);
361 process_bucket_arcs(b);
362
363 // iterate over fixed buckets and remove them
364 for (int b_prime = 0; b_prime < buckets_size; ++b_prime) {
365 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(b, b_prime)) {
366 buckets[b].template remove_bucket_arc<D>(b, b_prime);
367 }
368 }
369 }
370
371 // Print the total number of removed arcs.
372 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
373 print_info("[Fw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
374 else
375 print_info("[Bw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
376}

◆ calculate_neighborhoods()

void BucketGraph::calculate_neighborhoods ( size_t num_closest)

Calculates the neighborhoods for each node for the ng-routes.

Definition at line 285 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

285 {
286 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
287
288 // Resize the neighborhoods_bitmap vector so that there's one bitmap per
289 // node.
290 neighborhoods_bitmap.resize(num_nodes);
291
292 // Pre-calculate the number of segments needed (each segment is 64 bits).
293 const size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
294
295 // Process each node to compute its neighborhood.
296 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
297 // Create a vector of (distance, node_index) pairs for node i.
298 std::vector<std::pair<double, size_t>> forward_distances;
299 forward_distances.reserve(num_nodes - 1);
300
301 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
302 if (i == j) continue;
303 double distance = getcij(i, j);
304 forward_distances.push_back({distance, j});
305 }
306
307 // Sort distances to obtain the closest nodes first.
308 pdqsort(forward_distances.begin(), forward_distances.end(),
309 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.first < b.first; });
310
311 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmap for node i with all segments set
312 // to zero.
313 neighborhoods_bitmap[i].assign(num_segments, 0ULL);
314
315 // Always include the node itself in its neighborhood.
316 size_t self_segment = i >> 6;
317 size_t self_bit = i & 63;
318 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][self_segment] |= (1ULL << self_bit);
319
320 // Add the top 'num_closest' closest nodes as forward neighbors.
321 for (size_t k = 0; k < num_closest && k < forward_distances.size();
322 ++k) {
323 size_t neighbor_index = forward_distances[k].second;
324 size_t segment = neighbor_index >> 6;
325 size_t bit_position = neighbor_index & 63;
326 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
327 }
328 }
329}

◆ check_feasibility()

bool BucketGraph::check_feasibility ( const Label * fw_label,
const Label * bw_label )
inline

Checks the feasibility of a given forward and backward label.

This function determines if the transition from a forward label to a backward label is feasible based on resource constraints and node durations.

Definition at line 849 of file BucketGraph.h.

850 {
851 if (!fw_label || !bw_label) return false;
852
853 // Cache resources and node data.
854 const auto &fw_resources = fw_label->resources;
855 const auto &bw_resources = bw_label->resources;
856 const auto &node = nodes[fw_label->node_id];
857
858 const size_t n_resources = options.resources.size();
859
860 // Compute travel time only once.
861 const auto travel_time = getcij(fw_label->node_id, bw_label->node_id);
862
863 // Assuming the "time" resource is at a known index (e.g., index 0).
864 // If not, you can either iterate to find it once or precompute a
865 // boolean array.
866 if (n_resources > 0 && options.resources[0] == "time") {
867 // Check time feasibility.
868 if (numericutils::gt(fw_resources[0] + travel_time + node.duration,
869 bw_resources[0]))
870 return false;
871 } else {
872 // Fallback: iterate to check any resource named "time".
873 for (size_t r = 0; r < n_resources; ++r) {
874 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
875 if (numericutils::gt(
876 fw_resources[r] + travel_time + node.duration,
877 bw_resources[r]))
878 return false;
879 }
880 }
881 }
882
883 // Check additional resource feasibility (assuming resources[0] is
884 // "time") and additional resources (from index 1 onward) represent
885 // capacity constraints.
886 for (size_t i = 1; i < n_resources; ++i) {
887 if (fw_resources[i] + node.demand > bw_resources[i]) return false;
888 }
889
890 return true;
891 }

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::common_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph by clearing previous data and setting up forward and backward buckets.

1480 {
1481 // --- Pre-allocate and clear temporary vectors ---
1482 merged_labels.clear();
1483 merged_labels.reserve(50);
1484
1485 // Retrieve active cuts and build a vector of ActiveCutInfo.
1486 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1487 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
1488 std::vector<ActiveCutInfo> active_cuts_info;
1489 active_cuts_info.reserve(active_cuts.size());
1490 for (const auto &cut : active_cuts) {
1491 active_cuts_info.push_back(cut);
1492 }
1493
1494 // --- Sort arcs by scores for each node ---
1495 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
1496 for (auto &node : nodes) {
1497 if (!arc_scores[node.id].empty()) {
1498 node.sort_arcs_by_scores<D>(arc_scores[node.id], nodes,
1499 active_cuts_info);
1500 }
1501 arc_scores[node.id].clear();
1502 }
1503
1504 // --- Warm start: collect best labels from buckets ---
1505 auto &warm_labels = assign_buckets<D>(fw_warm_labels, bw_warm_labels);
1506 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
1507 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1508 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
1509 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
1510
1511 if (!merged_labels.empty() && options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1512 warm_labels.clear();
1513 warm_labels.reserve(buckets_size);
1514 for (int bucket = 0; bucket < buckets_size; ++bucket) {
1515 if (auto *label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label()) {
1516 warm_labels.push_back(label);
1517 }
1518 }
1519 }
1520
1521 // --- Initialize c_bar vector ---
1522 c_bar.resize(buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1523
1524 // --- Initialize dominance check counters for forward direction ---
1525 if constexpr (Direction::Forward == D) {
1526 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(buckets_size + 1, 0);
1527 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
1528 }
1529
1530 // --- Clear all buckets ---
1531 for (size_t b = 0; b < buckets_size; b++) {
1532 buckets[b].clear();
1533 }
1534
1535 // --- Warm start processing: sort warm_labels and compute reduced costs
1536 // ---
1537 if (options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1538 pdqsort(
1539 warm_labels.begin(), warm_labels.end(),
1540 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
1541
1542 std::vector<Label *> processed_labels;
1543 const size_t process_size = std::min(
1544 warm_labels.size(),
1545 static_cast<size_t>(options.n_warm_start * warm_labels.size()));
1546 for (size_t i = 0; i < process_size; ++i) {
1547 auto label = warm_labels[i];
1548 // Uncomment if you want to skip non-fresh labels.
1549 // if (!label->fresh) continue;
1550 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(label, D == Direction::Forward);
1551 if (new_label != nullptr) {
1552 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1553 fw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);
1554 } else {
1555 bw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);
1556 }
1557 processed_labels.push_back(new_label);
1558 }
1559 }
1560 warm_labels = std::move(processed_labels);
1561 }
1562
1563 // --- Prepare interval arrays and compute per-resource splits for depot
1564 // ---
1565 const size_t num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
1566 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
1567 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
1568 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
1569 // Compute per-resource split counts for the depot
1570 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // assuming node 0 is the depot
1571 std::vector<int> depot_splits(num_intervals);
1572 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1573 // Use the same ratio as earlier:
1574 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
1575 double node_range = VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r];
1576 depot_splits[r] =
1577 std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) *
1578 intervals[r].interval)));
1579 // Compute the base interval for resource r.
1580 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / depot_splits[r];
1581 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
1582 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
1583 }
1584
1585 // --- Interval-based bucket generation ---
1586 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
1587 int offset = 0;
1588 // Lambda for generating interval combinations and creating depots.
1589 auto generate_combinations = [&](bool is_forward, int &offset) {
1590 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
1591 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
1592 const auto depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
1593 const int calculated_index_base =
1594 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
1595 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
1596
1597 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
1598 // Recursive lambda to generate all interval combinations.
1599 std::function<void(int)> process_intervals = [&](int depth) {
1600 if (depth == num_intervals) {
1601 // Acquire a new depot label.
1602 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
1603 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
1604 for (int r = 0; r < static_cast<int>(num_intervals); ++r) {
1605 // For forward direction, start at the lower bound and
1606 // add multiples of the base interval; adjust similarly
1607 // for backward.
1608 interval_bounds[r] =
1609 is_forward ? interval_starts[r] +
1610 current_pos[r] * base_intervals[r]
1611 : interval_ends[r] -
1612 current_pos[r] * base_intervals[r];
1613 }
1614 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
1615 depot_id);
1616 depot->is_extended = false;
1617 depot->nodes_covered.push_back(depot_id);
1618 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
1619 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
1620 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
1621 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
1622 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
1623
1624 offset++;
1625 return;
1626 }
1627
1628 // For the current resource dimension, loop using its specific
1629 // split count.
1630 for (int k = 0; k < depot_splits[depth]; ++k) {
1631 current_pos[depth] = k;
1632 process_intervals(depth + 1);
1633 }
1634 };
1635 process_intervals(0);
1636 };
1637
1638 // Generate combinations for the current direction.
1639 generate_combinations(D == Direction::Forward, offset);
1640}
SRC_MODE_BLOCK
#define SRC_MODE_BLOCK(code_block)

1183 // Compute cost values
1184 double cij_cost = getcij(L->node_id, L_prime->node_id);

1190 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;

1199

1202 new_label->nodes_covered.begin() + L->nodes_covered.size());
1203
1204 return new_label;
1205}

◆ compute_mono_label()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_mono_label ( const Label * L)

Computes the mono label for the BucketGraph.

This function computes the mono label for the BucketGraph by acquiring a new label from the label pool and setting the cost and real cost values from the given label L. It then calculates the number of nodes covered by the label and its ancestors, reserves space for the nodes covered, and inserts the nodes from the

◆ compute_red_cost()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_red_cost ( Label * L,
bool fw )
inline

Definition at line 1180 of file BucketGraph.h.

1180 {
1181 // Calculate main and reduced costs.
1182 double real_cost = 0.0;
1183 double red_cost = 0.0;
1184
1185 // Check main resource feasibility.
1186 const double main_resource = L->resources[options.main_resources[0]];
1187 const double q_star_value = q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
1188 if (fw) {
1189 if (main_resource > q_star_value) return nullptr;
1190 } else {
1191 if (main_resource <= q_star_value) return nullptr;
1192 }
1193
1194 // Ensure sufficient nodes in the route.
1195 if (L->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) return nullptr;
1196
1197 // Initialize an empty SRCmap vector with size equal to the current
1198 // number of cuts.
1199 std::vector<uint16_t> updated_SRCmap(cut_storage->size(), 0);
1200
1201 // Prepare new label.
1202 auto new_label = new Label();
1203 new_label->nodes_covered.clear();
1204 new_label->nodes_covered = L->nodes_covered; // Copy the route
1205
1206 // Variables to keep track of costs.
1207 int last_node = -1;
1208
1209 // Traverse the nodes in the route (from current label to root).
1210 for (auto node_id : L->nodes_covered) {
1211 // --- NG-route Feasibility Check ---
1212 // Clear visited bitmap in the neighborhood of node_id.
1213 for (size_t seg = 0, limit = new_label->visited_bitmap.size();
1214 seg < limit; ++seg) {
1215 uint64_t current_visited = new_label->visited_bitmap[seg];
1216 if (!current_visited) continue;
1217 uint64_t neighborhood_mask = neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][seg];
1218 new_label->visited_bitmap[seg] =
1219 current_visited & neighborhood_mask;
1220 }
1221 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
1222
1223 // --- Cost Accumulation ---
1224 if (last_node != -1) {
1225 double cij_cost = getcij(last_node, node_id);
1226 real_cost += cij_cost;
1227 red_cost += cij_cost;
1228 }
1229 red_cost -= nodes[node_id].cost;
1230
1231 // --- SRC Logic ---
1232 size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
1233 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[node_id & 63];
1234 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1235 auto &SRCDuals = cutter->SRCDuals;
1236
1237#if defined(SRC)
1238 for (std::size_t idx = 0; idx < cutter->size(); ++idx) {
1239 const auto &cut = cutter->getCut(idx);
1240 // Retrieve base set and order information.
1241 const auto &baseSet = cut.baseSet;
1242 const auto &baseSetOrder = cut.baseSetOrder;
1243 const auto &neighbors = cut.neighbors;
1244 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
1245
1246 // If the current node is not in the neighbor set, reset SRCmap
1247 // value.
1248 if (!(neighbors[segment] & bit_mask)) {
1249 updated_SRCmap[idx] = 0;
1250 continue;
1251 }
1252 // If the current node is in the base set, update SRC value.
1253 if (baseSet[segment] & bit_mask) {
1254 auto &src_val = updated_SRCmap[idx];
1255 src_val += multipliers.num[baseSetOrder[node_id]];
1256 if (src_val >= multipliers.den) {
1257 red_cost -= SRCDuals[idx];
1258 src_val -= multipliers.den;
1259 }
1260 }
1261 }
1262#endif
1263
1264 // --- Arc and Branching Dual Adjustments ---
1265#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
1266 if (last_node != -1) {
1267 red_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(last_node, node_id);
1268 }
1269#endif
1270 if (branching_duals->size() > 0 && last_node != -1) {
1271 red_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(last_node, node_id);
1272 }
1273
1274 last_node = node_id; // Update last_node.
1275 }
1276
1277 // Set final label properties.
1278 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1279 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1280 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1281 new_label->node_id = L->node_id;
1282 new_label->is_extended = false;
1283 new_label->resources = L->resources;
1284 new_label->SRCmap = updated_SRCmap;
1285 new_label->fresh = false;
1286
1287 // Determine bucket number for the new label.
1288 std::vector<double> new_resources(options.resources.size());
1289 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
1290 new_resources[i] = new_label->resources[i];
1291 }
1292 int bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(
1293 new_label->node_id, new_resources)
1294 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(
1295 new_label->node_id, new_resources);
1296 new_label->vertex = bucket;
1297
1298 return new_label;
1299 }
Direction
Direction

BucketGraph::branching_duals
BranchingDualsPtr branching_duals

◆ computePhi()

std::vector< int > BucketGraph::computePhi ( int & bucket_id,
bool fw )

Computes the phi values for a given bucket ID and direction.

Definition at line 196 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

196 {
197 std::vector<int> phi;
198
199 // Get references based on the direction.
200 auto &buckets = fw ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
201 auto &node_interval_trees =
202 fw ? fw_node_interval_trees : bw_node_interval_trees;
203
204 // Multi-dimensional resource case.
205 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
206 if (bucket_id < 0 || bucket_id >= static_cast<int>(buckets.size()))
207 return phi;
208
209 const int n_dims = static_cast<int>(intervals.size());
210 std::vector<double> base_intervals(n_dims, 0.0);
211
212 // Get the node associated with the current bucket.
213 int node_id = buckets[bucket_id].node_id;
214 const VRPNode &node = nodes[node_id];
215
216 // Recompute splits and base intervals per dimension using the same
217 // logic as in define_buckets.
218 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
219 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
220 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
221 int splits = 1;
222 if (std::fabs(full_range) >
223 std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
224 double splits_d =
225 (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
226 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
227 }
228 base_intervals[r] = node_range / static_cast<double>(splits);
229 }
230
231 // Start with the current bucket's baseline.
232 const auto &current_bucket = buckets[bucket_id];
233 std::vector<double> target = fw ? current_bucket.lb : current_bucket.ub;
234
235 // Adjust the target vector by one base interval for each dimension.
236 // For forward, shift left (i.e. subtract) but do not go below the
237 // node's lb. For backward, shift right (i.e. add) but do not exceed the
238 // node's ub.
239 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
240 if (fw)
241 target[r] = std::max(node.lb[r], target[r] - base_intervals[r]);
242 else
243 target[r] = std::min(node.ub[r], target[r] + base_intervals[r]);
244 }
245
246 // Look up the candidate bucket in the node's interval tree.
247 TreeNode *found_node = node_interval_trees[node_id].find(target);
248 if (found_node != nullptr &&
249 buckets[found_node->bucket_index].node_id == node_id) {
250#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
251 bool not_fixed = fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(
252 bucket_id, found_node->bucket_index)
253 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(
254 bucket_id, found_node->bucket_index);
255 if (not_fixed)
256#endif
257 {
258 phi.push_back(found_node->bucket_index);
259 }
260 }
261 }
262 // Single-resource case (simpler).
263 else {
264 int neighbor = fw ? bucket_id - 1 : bucket_id - 1;
265 if (neighbor >= 0 && neighbor < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()) &&
266 buckets[neighbor].node_id == buckets[bucket_id].node_id) {
267#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
268 bool not_fixed =
269 fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(neighbor, bucket_id)
270 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(neighbor, bucket_id);
271 if (not_fixed)
272#endif
273 {
274 phi.push_back(neighbor);
275 }
276 }
277 }
278 return phi;
279}

template<Stage S, Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::ConcatenateLabel ( const Label * L,
int & b,
std::atomic< double > & best_cost )

Concatenates the label L with the bucket b and updates the best label pbest.

656 {
657 // Determine the number of segments needed for the bucket visited
658 // bitmap.
659 const size_t n_segments = (fw_buckets_size + 63) / 64;
660 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
661
662 // Use thread_local storage for the bucket stack to avoid reallocations.
663 static thread_local std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
664 bucket_stack.reserve(R_SIZE); // Reserve once at initialization
665 bucket_stack.clear(); // Clear for reuse
666 bucket_stack.push_back(b);
667
668 // Choose symmetric buckets and cost-bar arrays based on symmetry.
669 auto &other_buckets = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
670 auto &other_c_bar = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
671
672 // Cache frequently accessed values.
673 const int L_node_id = L->node_id;
674 const auto &L_resources = L->resources;
675 const auto &L_last_node = nodes[L_node_id];
676 const double L_cost = L->cost;
677 const size_t bitmap_size = L->visited_bitmap.size();
678
679 // Pre-compute if branching is active.
680 const bool has_branching = !branching_duals->empty();
681
682#if defined(SRC)
683 // SRC mode setup: Only relevant in Stage::Four.
684 decltype(cut_storage) cutter = nullptr;
685 if constexpr (S > Stage::Three) {
686 cutter = cut_storage;
687 }
688 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
689#endif
690
691 // Lambda to check if the visited bitmaps of L and L_bw overlap.
692 auto overlapsVisited = [bitmap_size, this](const Label *L,
693 const Label *L_bw) -> bool {
694 // Make a local copy of the visited bitmap.
695 auto visited_bitmap = L->visited_bitmap;
696
697 // check if visited_bitmap overlaps
698 // for (size_t i = 0; i < bitmap_size; ++i) {
699 // if (visited_bitmap[i] & L_bw->visited_bitmap[i]) {
700 // return true;
701 // }
702 // }
703 // // Iterate in reverse over nodes_covered without allocating a new
704 // vector.
705 for (auto it = L_bw->nodes_covered.rbegin();
706 it != L_bw->nodes_covered.rend(); ++it) {
707 uint16_t node_id = *it;
708 if (node_id == L->node_id ||
709 is_node_visited(visited_bitmap, node_id))
710 return true;
711 // Update the bitmap for each 64-bit word.
712 for (size_t i = 0; i < visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
713 uint64_t &current_visited = visited_bitmap[i];
714 if (current_visited != 0)
715 current_visited &= neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i];
716 }
717 set_node_visited(visited_bitmap, node_id);
718 }
719 return false;
720 };
721
722// Lambda to apply SRC adjustments to total_cost (only for Stage::Four).
723#if defined(SRC)
724 auto applySRCAdjustments = [&](double total_cost, const Label *L,
725 const Label *L_bw) -> double {
726 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
727 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
728 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
729 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
730 const double dual = active_cut.dual_value;
731 if (L->SRCmap[idx] + L_bw->SRCmap[idx] >= cut.p.den) {
732 total_cost -= dual;
733 }
734 }
735 }
736 return total_cost;
737 };
738#endif
739
740 // Process buckets until the stack is empty.
741 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
742 const int current_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
743 bucket_stack.pop_back();
744
745 // Mark current_bucket as visited in the bitmap.
746 const size_t segment = current_bucket >> 6;
747 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[current_bucket & 63];
748 Bvisited[segment] |= bit_mask;
749
750 // Get the node id associated with this bucket.
751 const int bucketLprimenode = other_buckets[current_bucket].node_id;
752 double travel_cost = getcij(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
753
754#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
755 // Adjust travel cost with arc duals in Stage Four.
756 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
757 travel_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
758 }
759#endif
760
761 if (has_branching) {
762 travel_cost -=
763 branching_duals->getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
764 }
765
766 const double path_cost = L_cost + travel_cost;
767 const double bound = other_c_bar[current_bucket];
768
769 // Early bound check.
770 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate &&
771 numericutils::gte(path_cost + bound, best_cost.load())) ||
772 (S == Stage::Enumerate &&
773 numericutils::gte(path_cost + bound, gap))) {
774 continue;
775 }
776
777 // Retrieve labels in the current bucket.
778 const auto &bucket = other_buckets[current_bucket];
779 const auto labels = bucket.get_labels();
780 if (labels.empty()) continue;
781
782 // Process each label in the current bucket.
783 for (const Label *L_bw : labels) {
784 // Skip invalid labels with combined check
785 if (L_bw == nullptr || L_bw->is_dominated ||
786 L_bw->node_id == L_node_id) {
787 continue;
788 }
789
790 // Check feasibility
791 if (!check_feasibility(L, L_bw)) {
792 continue;
793 }
794
795 double total_cost = path_cost + L_bw->cost;
796
797#if defined(SRC)
798 // Apply SRC adjustments if in Stage Four.
799 total_cost = applySRCAdjustments(total_cost, L, L_bw);
800#endif
801 double latest_best = best_cost.load();
802
803 // Filter based on total cost against best known cost or gap.
804 bool cost_acceptable;
805 if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
806 cost_acceptable = numericutils::lt(total_cost, latest_best);
807 } else {
808 cost_acceptable = numericutils::lt(total_cost, gap);
809 }
810
811 if (!cost_acceptable) {
812 continue;
813 }
814 // Use critical section only when necessary
815 {
816 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(merge_mutex);
817
818 // Use atomic compare-exchange loop with exponential backoff
819 double expected = best_cost.load();
820 int backoff_counter = 0;
821
822 while (numericutils::lt(total_cost, expected) &&
823 !best_cost.compare_exchange_weak(expected, total_cost)) {
824 // Simple exponential backoff to reduce contention
825 if (++backoff_counter > 3) {
826 for (int i = 0; i < (1 << (backoff_counter - 3)); ++i) {
827 _mm_pause(); // CPU hint to reduce power in spin
828 // loops
829 }
830 }
831 }
832
833 // Add label if still an improvement
834 if (numericutils::lte(total_cost, best_cost.load())) {
835 auto pbest = compute_label<S>(L, L_bw, total_cost);
836 merged_labels.push_back(pbest);
837 }
838 }
839 }
840 // Add neighbor buckets to the stack if they have not been visited.
841 for (int b_prime : Phi_bw[current_bucket]) {
842 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
843 const uint64_t mask_prime = bit_mask_lookup[b_prime & 63];
844 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & mask_prime)) {
845 bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
846 }
847 }
848 }
849}

◆ define_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( )

Defines the buckets for the BucketGraph.

This function determines the number of buckets based on the time intervals and assigns buckets to the graph. It computes resource bounds for each vertex and defines the bounds of each bucket.

Definition at line 174 of file BucketUtils.h.

174 {
175 // 1. Save the old buckets and fixed status.
176 std::vector<Bucket> old_buckets;
177 std::vector<bool> was_fixed;
178 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
179 size_t old_size = buckets.size();
180
181 // Map to store the fixed range (min_lb and max_ub) for each node pair.
182 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<
183 int, ankerl::unordered_dense::map<
184 int, std::pair<std::vector<double>, std::vector<double>>>>
185 node_pair_fixed_ranges;
186
187 if (old_size > 0) {
188 old_buckets = buckets;
189 was_fixed.resize(old_size * old_size, false);
190 auto &old_bitmap =
191 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
192
193 // Mark fixed bucket pairs.
194 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
195 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
196 size_t pos = i * old_size + j;
197 if (pos / 64 < old_bitmap.size()) {
198 was_fixed[pos] =
199 ((old_bitmap[pos / 64] & (1ULL << (pos % 64))) != 0);
200 }
201 }
202 }
203
204 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
205 // For each fixed bucket pair, update the fixed range for the
206 // corresponding node pair.
207 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
208 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
209 if (!was_fixed[i * old_size + j]) continue;
210 const auto &bucket_from = old_buckets[i];
211 const auto &bucket_to = old_buckets[j];
212 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
213 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
214 // Initialize if necessary.
215 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
216 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end()) {
217 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node] = {
218 std::vector<double>(num_intervals,
219 std::numeric_limits<double>::max()),
220 std::vector<double>(
221 num_intervals,
222 std::numeric_limits<double>::lowest())};
223 }
224 auto &[min_lbs, max_ubs] =
225 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
226 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
227 min_lbs[r] = std::min(min_lbs[r], bucket_from.lb[r]);
228 max_ubs[r] = std::max(max_ubs[r], bucket_from.ub[r]);
229 }
230 }
231 }
232 } // End if old buckets exist.
233
234 // 2. Clear the current buckets and fixed bitmap.
235 buckets.clear();
236 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
237 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
238 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
239
240 // 3. Prepare variables and containers for new bucket definition.
241 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
242 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals); // unused in current code
243 std::vector<double> base_intervals(
244 num_intervals); // unused in current code
245
246 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
247 auto &num_buckets_index =
248 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
249 auto &node_interval_trees =
250 assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
251 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
252 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
253
254 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
255 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
256 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
257 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
258
259 // Lambda to calculate an interval for one dimension.
260 auto calculate_interval =
261 [&](double lb, double ub, double base_interval, int pos,
262 int max_intervals, bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
263 double start, end;
264 if (is_forward) {
265 // For the first interval, start exactly at lb.
266 start = (pos == 0) ? lb : lb + pos * base_interval;
267 // For the last interval, end exactly at ub.
268 end = (pos == max_intervals - 1) ? ub
269 : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
270 } else {
271 // For Backward: first interval gets ub exactly, last gets lb
272 // exactly.
273 start = (pos == max_intervals - 1) ? lb
274 : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
275 end = (pos == 0) ? ub : ub - pos * base_interval;
276 }
277 start = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(start);
278 end = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(end);
279 return {start, end};
280 };
281
282 int cum_sum = 0;
283 int bucket_index = 0;
284 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
285 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
286 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals,
287 0); // combination indices for multi-dimensions
288
289 // 4. Process each node to define new buckets.
290 for (const auto &vrp_node : nodes) {
291 std::vector<int> node_split_counts(num_intervals);
292 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
293 // Determine the number of splits and base interval for each
294 // resource dimension.
295 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
296 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
297 double node_range = vrp_node.ub[r] - vrp_node.lb[r];
298 int splits = 1;
299 if (std::fabs(full_range) >
300 std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
301 double splits_d =
302 (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
303 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
304 }
305 node_split_counts[r] = splits;
306 node_base_interval[r] =
307 (vrp_node.ub[r] - vrp_node.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
308 }
309 // Optionally store node-specific split counts.
310 bucket_splits[vrp_node.id] = node_split_counts;
311
312 int n_buckets = 0;
313 if (num_intervals == 1) {
314 // Single-dimensional case.
315 for (int j = 0; j < node_split_counts[0]; ++j) {
316 auto [int_start, int_end] = calculate_interval(
317 vrp_node.lb[0], vrp_node.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
318 node_split_counts[0], D == Direction::Forward);
319 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward)
320 int_start = std::max(int_start, vrp_node.lb[0]);
321 else
322 int_end = std::min(int_end, vrp_node.ub[0]);
323 buckets.push_back(Bucket(vrp_node.id,
324 std::vector<double>{int_start},
325 std::vector<double>{int_end}));
326 n_buckets++;
327 cum_sum++;
328 }
329 } else {
330 // Multi-dimensional: iterate over all combinations.
331 fmt::print("Multiple intervals\n");
332 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
333 while (true) {
334 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
335 auto [int_start, int_end] = calculate_interval(
336 vrp_node.lb[r], vrp_node.ub[r], node_base_interval[r],
337 pos[r], node_split_counts[r], D == Direction::Forward);
338 interval_start[r] = int_start;
339 interval_end[r] = int_end;
340 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward)
341 interval_start[r] =
342 std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
343 else
344 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
345 }
346 buckets.push_back(
347 Bucket(vrp_node.id, interval_start, interval_end));
348 n_buckets++;
349 cum_sum++;
350
351 // Generate next combination.
352 int i = 0;
353 while (i < num_intervals) {
354 ++pos[i];
355 if (pos[i] < node_split_counts[i])
356 break;
357 else {
358 pos[i] = 0;
359 ++i;
360 }
361 }
362 if (i == num_intervals) break;
363 }
364 }
365 num_buckets[vrp_node.id] = n_buckets;
366 num_buckets_index[vrp_node.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
367 // Optionally assign node_interval_trees[vrp_node.id] = node_tree;
368 //
369 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
370 n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
371 }
372 }
373
374 // 5. Process fixed bucket ranges (if available).
375 if (!node_pair_fixed_ranges.empty()) {
376 size_t new_size = buckets.size();
377 size_t required_bitmap_words =
378 std::max(size_t(1), ((new_size * new_size) + 63) / 64);
379 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
380 fixed_buckets_bitmap.resize(required_bitmap_words, 0);
381 for (size_t from = 0; from < new_size; ++from) {
382 const auto &bucket_from = buckets[from];
383 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
384 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges.find(from_node) ==
385 node_pair_fixed_ranges.end())
386 continue;
387 for (size_t to = 0; to < new_size; ++to) {
388 const auto &bucket_to = buckets[to];
389 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
390 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
391 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end())
392 continue;
393 const auto &range = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
394 bool in_range = true;
395 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
396 if (bucket_from.lb[r] < range.first[r] ||
397 bucket_from.ub[r] > range.second[r]) {
398 in_range = false;
399 break;
400 }
401 }
402 if (in_range) {
403 size_t bit_pos = from * new_size + to;
404 size_t word_idx = bit_pos / 64;
405 if (word_idx < fixed_buckets_bitmap.size()) {
406 fixed_buckets_bitmap[word_idx] |=
407 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
408 }
409 }
410 }
411 }
412 }
413
414 // 6. Finalize: record the total bucket count.
415 buckets_size = buckets.size();
416}
roundToTwoDecimalPlaces

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( const int fixed_num_buckets)
inline

1409 {
1410 // Get references to forward/backward buckets.
1411 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1412 buckets.clear();
1413
1414 // Also clear the fixed bitmap (if used elsewhere).
1415 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =

1418

1422 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
1423 auto &num_buckets_index =
1424 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
1425 auto &node_interval_trees =
1426 assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
1427 auto &buckets_size =
1428 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
1429 auto &bucket_splits =
1430 assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
1431
1432 // Pre-allocate per-node containers.
1433 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
1434 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
1435 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
1436 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
1437
1438 // Lambda to calculate an interval for a given resource dimension.
1439 // Rounding is applied after computing boundaries.
1440 auto calculate_interval =
1441 [&](double lb, double ub, double base_interval, int pos,
1442 int max_interval,
1443 bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
1444 double start, end;
1445 if (is_forward) {
1446 start = (pos == 0) ? lb : lb + pos * base_interval;
1447 end = (pos == max_interval - 1)
1448 ? ub
1449 : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
1450 } else {
1451 start = (pos == max_interval - 1)
1452 ? lb
1453 : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
1454 end = (pos == 0) ? ub : ub - pos * base_interval;
1455 }
1456 return {roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(start),
1457 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(end)};
1458 };
1459
1460 int bucket_index = 0;
1461 int cum_sum = 0;
1462 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
1463 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
1464 // "pos" holds the current combination indices when
1465 // multi-dimensional.
1466 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals, 0);
1467
1468 // Process each node (job) to define its buckets.
1469 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
1470 // Compute the number of splits for each resource dimension.
1471 // For a single dimension, we use the fixed number directly.
1472 // For multiple dimensions we choose an equal split in each
1473 // dimension.
1474 std::vector<int> node_split_counts(num_intervals, 1);
1475 if (num_intervals == 1) {
1476 node_split_counts[0] = fixed_num_buckets;
1477 } else {
1478 // Compute an equal split count per dimension (rounded).
1479 int equal_split =
1480 std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(std::pow(
1481 fixed_num_buckets, 1.0 / num_intervals))));
1482 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1483 node_split_counts[r] = equal_split;
1484 }
1485 // Adjust the first dimension if the product is less than
1486 // fixed_num_buckets.
1487 int product = 1;
1488 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1489 product *= node_split_counts[r];
1490 }
1491 if (product < fixed_num_buckets) {
1492 int factor = fixed_num_buckets / product;
1493 node_split_counts[0] *= factor;
1494 }
1495 }
1496
1497 // Compute the width (base interval) for each resource
1498 // dimension.
1499 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
1500 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1501 node_base_interval[r] =
1502 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) /
1503 static_cast<double>(node_split_counts[r]);
1504 }
1505
1506 // Optionally store the per-node split information.
1507 // (Here we store just the first dimension's split count; you
1508 // could store the full vector.)
1509 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id] = (num_intervals == 1)
1510 ? node_split_counts[0]
1511 : node_split_counts[0];
1512
1513 SplayTree node_tree;
1514 int n_buckets = 0;
1515
1516 if (num_intervals == 1) {
1517 // Single-dimensional splitting.
1518 for (int j = 0; j < node_split_counts[0]; ++j) {
1519 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
1520 VRPNode.lb[0], VRPNode.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
1521 node_split_counts[0], D == Direction::Forward);
1522 // Clamp to the node's bounds.
1523 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
1524 start = std::max(start, VRPNode.lb[0]);
1525 } else {
1526 end = std::min(end, VRPNode.ub[0]);
1527 }
1528 buckets.push_back(Bucket(VRPNode.id,
1529 std::vector<double>{start},
1530 std::vector<double>{end}));
1531 node_tree.insert(std::vector<double>{start},
1532 std::vector<double>{end}, bucket_index++);
1533 n_buckets++;
1534 cum_sum++;
1535 }
1536 } else {
1537 // Multi-dimensional splitting.
1538 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
1539 while (true) {
1540 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1541 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
1542 VRPNode.lb[r], VRPNode.ub[r], node_base_interval[r],
1543 pos[r], node_split_counts[r],
1544 D == Direction::Forward);
1545 interval_start[r] = start;
1546 interval_end[r] = end;
1547 // Optionally clamp to global resource bounds.
1548 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
1549 interval_start[r] =
1550 std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
1551 } else {
1552 interval_end[r] =
1553 std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
1554 }
1555 }
1556 buckets.push_back(
1557 Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
1558 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end,
1559 bucket_index++);
1560 n_buckets++;
1561 cum_sum++;
1562
1563 // Generate next combination of splits.
1564 int i = 0;
1565 while (i < num_intervals) {
1566 ++pos[i];
1567 if (pos[i] < node_split_counts[i]) {
1568 break;
1569 } else {
1570 pos[i] = 0;
1571 ++i;
1572 }
1573 }
1574 if (i == num_intervals) break;
1575 }
1576 }
1577
1578 // Update per-node bucket bookkeeping.
1579 num_buckets[VRPNode.id] = n_buckets;
1580 num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
1581 node_interval_trees[VRPNode.id] = node_tree;
1582 }
1583
1584 // Update the overall bucket count.
1585 buckets_size = buckets.size();
1586 }
SplayTree::insert
void insert(const std::vector< double > &low, const std::vector< double > &high, int bucket_index)

void BucketGraph::define_elementarity_sets ( )
inline

Definition at line 406 of file BucketGraph.h.

406 {
407 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
408
409 // Initialize elementarity sets
410 elementarity_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for elementarity sets
411
412 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
413 // Each customer should appear in its own elementarity
414 // set
415 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
416 elementarity_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
417
418 // Mark the node itself in the elementarity set
419 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
420 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
421 elementarity_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
422 }
423 }

425 {
426 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
427
428 // Initialize packing sets
429 packing_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for packing sets
430
431 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
432 // Each customer should appear in its own packing set
433 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
434 packing_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
435
436 // Mark the node itself in the packing set
437 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
438 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
439 packing_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
440 }
441 }

◆ DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets ( const Label *__restrict__ L,
int bucket,
const std::vector< double > &__restrict__ c_bar,
std::vector< uint64_t > &__restrict__ Bvisited,
int & stat_n_dom )
inlinenoexcept

Determines if a label is dominated in component-wise smaller buckets.

This function checks if a given label is dominated by any other label in component-wise smaller buckets. The dominance is determined based on the cost and order of the buckets.

Definition at line 1037 of file BucketSolve.h.

1040 {
1041 // Cache direction-specific data
1042 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1043 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
1044 const auto &uf = assign_buckets<D>(fw_union_find, bw_union_find);
1045 auto &union_cache = assign_buckets<D>(fw_union_cache, bw_union_cache);
1046
1047 // Cache frequently used label properties
1048 const int b_L = L->vertex;
1049 const double label_cost = L->cost;
1050 const size_t res_size = options.resources.size();
1051
1052 // Use static thread-local stack to avoid repeated allocations
1053 static thread_local std::vector<int> stack_buffer;
1054 if (stack_buffer.capacity() < R_SIZE) {
1055 stack_buffer.reserve(R_SIZE);
1056 }
1057 stack_buffer.clear();
1058 stack_buffer.push_back(bucket);
1059
1060 // Direct pointer to bit mask lookup for fast bit operations
1061 const uint64_t *const bit_mask_lookup_ptr = bit_mask_lookup.data();
1062
1063 // Inline dominance check function to avoid lambda overhead
1064 auto inline_check_dominance = [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels,
1065 int &n_dom) -> bool {
1066#ifdef __AVX2__
1067 if (labels.size() >= AVX_LIM)
1068 return check_dominance_against_vector<D, S>(L, labels, cut_storage,
1069 res_size, n_dom);
1070#endif
1071 const size_t size = labels.size();
1072 size_t i = 0;
1073 // Loop unrolling: process 4 labels at a time
1074 for (; i + 3 < size; i += 4) {
1075 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i]) ||
1076 is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i + 1]) ||
1077 is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i + 2]) ||
1078 is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i + 3])) {
1079 ++n_dom;
1080 return true;
1081 }
1082 }
1083 // Process remaining labels
1084 for (; i < size; ++i) {
1085 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i])) {
1086 ++n_dom;
1087 return true;
1088 }
1089 }
1090 return false;
1091 };
1092
1093 // Main DFS traversal loop
1094 while (!stack_buffer.empty()) {
1095 const int current_bucket = stack_buffer.back();
1096 stack_buffer.pop_back();
1097
1098 const size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(current_bucket) >> 6;
1099 const uint64_t bit = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[current_bucket & 63];
1100
1101 if (unlikely(Bvisited[segment] & bit)) continue;
1102
1103 // Mark as visited
1104 Bvisited[segment] |= bit;
1105
1106 // Fast path: if label cost is lower and bucket precedes L in the SCC
1107 // order, return early
1108 if (likely(label_cost < c_bar[current_bucket] &&
1109 ::precedes(current_bucket, b_L, uf, union_cache)))
1110 return false;
1111
1112 // Skip dominance check for L's own bucket
1113 if (b_L != current_bucket) {
1114 const auto &mother_bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1115 if (mother_bucket.check_dominance(L, inline_check_dominance,
1116 stat_n_dom))
1117 return true;
1118 }
1119
1120 // Process neighbor buckets using pointer arithmetic and prefetching
1121 const auto &neighbors = Phi[current_bucket];
1122 const size_t n_neighbors = neighbors.size();
1123 const int *neighbor_ptr = neighbors.data();
1124 const int *neighbor_end = neighbor_ptr + n_neighbors;
1125 for (; neighbor_ptr != neighbor_end; ++neighbor_ptr) {
1126 const int b_prime = *neighbor_ptr;
1127 const size_t word_idx = static_cast<size_t>(b_prime) >> 6;
1128 const uint64_t mask = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[b_prime & 63];
1129 __builtin_prefetch(&Bvisited[word_idx], 0,
1130 1); // Prefetch Bvisited for this segment
1131 if (!(Bvisited[word_idx] & mask)) stack_buffer.push_back(b_prime);
1132 }
1133 }
1134
1135 return false;
1136}

◆ Extend()

template<Direction D, Stage S, ArcType A, Mutability M, Full F>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::Extend ( const std::conditional_t< M==Mutability::Mut, Label *, const Label * > L_prime,
const std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Bucket, BucketArc, std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Jump, JumpArc, Arc > > & gamma,
std::vector< Label * > * output_buffer = nullptr,
int depth = 0 )
inlinenoexcept

Extends the label L_prime with the given BucketArc gamma.

Definition at line 621 of file BucketSolve.h.

627 {
628 // Pre-allocated result vector to avoid allocations for the common case of
629 // 0-1 results
630 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> result_vector;
631 std::vector<Label *> &result =
632 output_buffer ? *output_buffer : result_vector;
633 result.clear();
634
635 // Prepare temporary resource storage (resize to current resources size)
636 static thread_local std::vector<double> new_resources;
637 const size_t n_resources = options.resources.size();
638 new_resources.resize(n_resources);
639
640 // Cache initial values from L_prime
641 const int initial_node_id = L_prime->node_id;
642 const auto &initial_resources =
643 L_prime->resources; // Use reference instead of copy
644 const double initial_cost = L_prime->cost;
645
646 // Determine target node ID based on arc type
647 int node_id;
648 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
649 node_id =
650 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets)[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
651 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
652 node_id = gamma.to;
653 } else { // Jump arc
654 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
655 node_id = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket].node_id;
656 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
657
658 // Fast copy the resources
659 std::memcpy(new_resources.data(), initial_resources.data(),
660 n_resources * sizeof(double));
661
662 // Update resource bounds based on jump bucket properties
663 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_resources; ++i) {
664 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
665 new_resources[i] = std::max(new_resources[i],
666 static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
667 } else {
668 new_resources[i] = std::min(new_resources[i],
669 static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
670 }
671 }
672 }
673
674 // Early rejection: if we loop back or the node was already visited
675 if (node_id == initial_node_id ||
676 is_node_visited(L_prime->visited_bitmap, node_id))
677 return result;
678
679 // For stages where fixed arcs matter
680 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
681 if ((D == Direction::Forward &&
682 is_arc_fixed(initial_node_id, node_id)) ||
683 (D == Direction::Backward &&
684 is_arc_fixed(node_id, initial_node_id)))
685 return result;
686 }
687
688 // Process resources (feasibility check)
689 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
690 if constexpr (A != ArcType::Jump) {
691 // Only copy resources for non-jump arcs (jump arcs already copied)
692 std::memcpy(new_resources.data(), initial_resources.data(),
693 n_resources * sizeof(double));
694
695 if (!process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, initial_resources,
696 gamma, nodes[node_id], n_resources))
697 return result;
698 } else {
699 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
700 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
701 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_resources; ++i) {
702 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
703 new_resources[i] =
704 std::max(new_resources[i] + gamma.resource_increment[i],
705 static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
706 if (numericutils::gt(new_resources[i], bucket.ub[i])) {
707 return {};
708 }
709
710 } else {
711 new_resources[i] =
712 std::min(new_resources[i] - gamma.resource_increment[i],
713 static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
714 if (numericutils::lt(new_resources[i], bucket.lb[i])) {
715 return {};
716 }
717 }
718 }
719 }
720 }
721
722 // For PSTEP/TSP: Check path length constraints
723 if constexpr (F == Full::PSTEP || F == Full::TSP) {
724 int n_visited = 0;
725 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap)
726 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
727 if (n_visited > options.max_path_size ||
728 (n_visited == options.max_path_size &&
729 node_id != options.end_depot))
730 return result;
731 }
732
733 // Get bucket number for the new label
734 int to_bucket = -1;
735 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
736 to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
737 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Jump) {
738 to_bucket = gamma.jump_bucket;
739 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
740 to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
741 }
742
743 // #ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
744 // // If bucket is fixed (for Stage::Four) and not a jump arc, try jump
745 // arcs
746 // // recursively
747 // if constexpr (S == Stage::Four && A != ArcType::Jump &&
748 // A != ArcType::Bucket) {
749 // if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(L_prime->vertex, to_bucket)) {
750 // static thread_local std::vector<Label *> jump_result;
751 // jump_result.clear();
752
753 // auto jump_arcs =
754 // nodes[initial_node_id].template
755 // get_jump_arcs<D>(node_id);
756 // for (const auto &jump_arc : jump_arcs) {
757 // auto extended_labels =
758 // Extend<D, S, ArcType::Jump, Mutability::Const, F>(
759 // L_prime, jump_arc, &jump_result, depth + 1);
760
761 // if (!extended_labels.empty()) {
762 // result.insert(result.end(), extended_labels.begin(),
763 // extended_labels.end());
764 // }
765 // }
766 // return result;
767 // }
768 // }
769 // #endif
770
771 // Calculate new cost
772#ifdef CUSTOM_COST
773 const auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
774 double new_cost = cost_calculator.calculate_cost(initial_cost, distance,
775 node_id, new_resources);
776#else
777 // Cache the distance value to avoid repeated calls
778 const auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
779 double new_cost = initial_cost + distance;
780#endif
781
782 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
783
784 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
785 new_cost -= VRPNode.cost;
786 } else {
787 int n_visited = 0;
788 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap)
789 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
790 if (n_visited > 1 && initial_node_id != options.depot) {
791 const auto three_two_dual =
792 pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(initial_node_id);
793 new_cost += three_two_dual +
794 pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(initial_node_id);
795 }
796 new_cost += -pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(node_id) +
797 pstep_duals.getArcDualValue(initial_node_id, node_id);
798 }
799
800 // Apply branching dual adjustments if present - use local variables to
801 // avoid repeated pointer dereferencing
802 if (!branching_duals->empty()) {
803 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
804 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(initial_node_id, node_id);
805 } else {
806 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
807 }
808 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id);
809 }
810
811 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
812 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
813 new_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(initial_node_id, node_id);
814 } else {
815 new_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
816 }
817 })
818
819 // Handle Reverse mode early
820 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
821 if (new_resources[options.main_resources[0]] <=
822 std::floor(q_star[options.main_resources[0]]))
823 return result;
824
825 auto new_label = new Label();
826 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
827 result.push_back(new_label);
828 return result;
829 }
830
831 // Create and initialize a new label
832 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
833 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
834 new_label->initialize(to_bucket, new_cost, new_resources, node_id);
835 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
836 new_label->real_cost =
837 L_prime->real_cost + distance; // Use cached distance
838
839 if constexpr (M == Mutability::Mut) {
840 L_prime->children.push_back(new_label);
841 }
842
843 // Update visited bitmap: intersect parent's visited bits with neighborhood
844 // mask
845 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
846 for (size_t i = 0; i < new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
847 const uint64_t current_visited = L_prime->visited_bitmap[i];
848 if (current_visited) {
849 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] =
850 current_visited & neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i];
851 }
852 }
853 } else {
854 // Use memcpy for direct bitmap copy (likely faster than assignment for
855 // large bitmaps)
856 std::memcpy(new_label->visited_bitmap.data(),
857 L_prime->visited_bitmap.data(),
858 new_label->visited_bitmap.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
859 }
860
861 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
862
863 // Update the path (nodes covered) by copying parent's path and adding
864 // current node
865 new_label->nodes_covered = L_prime->nodes_covered;
866 new_label->nodes_covered.push_back(node_id);
867
868#if defined(SRC)
869 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
870 new_label->SRCmap = L_prime->SRCmap;
871 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
872 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
873 const auto n_cuts = cutter->activeSize();
874
875 if (n_cuts == 0) {
876 result.push_back(new_label);
877 return result;
878 }
879
880 double total_cost_update = 0.0;
881 const size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
882 const size_t bit_position = node_id & 63;
883 auto &masks = cutter->getSegmentMasks();
884 const auto cut_limit_mask = masks.get_cut_limit_mask();
885
886 uint64_t valid_cuts = masks.get_valid_cut_mask(segment, bit_position);
887 valid_cuts &= cut_limit_mask; // Safeguard
888
889 // Process all valid cuts
890 while (valid_cuts) {
891 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(valid_cuts);
892 const auto &active_cut = active_cuts[cut_idx];
893 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
894 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[active_cut.index];
895 src_map_value += cut.p.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
896
897 if (src_map_value >= cut.p.den) {
898 src_map_value -= cut.p.den;
899 total_cost_update -= active_cut.dual_value;
900 }
901
902 valid_cuts &= (valid_cuts - 1); // Clear the least significant bit
903 }
904
905 // Process cuts to clear
906 uint64_t to_clear =
907 (~masks.get_neighbor_mask(segment, bit_position)) & cut_limit_mask;
908 while (to_clear) {
909 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(to_clear);
910 new_label->SRCmap[active_cuts[cut_idx].index] = 0.0;
911 to_clear &= (to_clear - 1); // Clear the least significant bit
912 }
913
914 new_label->cost += total_cost_update;
915 }
916#endif
917
918 result.push_back(new_label);
919 return result;
920}
RCC_MODE_BLOCK
#define RCC_MODE_BLOCK(code_block)

Definition BucketGraph.h:301

◆ forbidCycle()

void BucketGraph::forbidCycle ( const std::vector< int > & cycle,
bool aggressive )

Forbids a cycle in the bucket graph.

This function takes a vector representing a cycle in the graph and forbids the edges corresponding to the cycle. If the 'aggressive' flag is set to true, it also forbids additional edges between the vertices of the cycle.

Definition at line 521 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

521 {
522 // If the cycle is too short, there is nothing to forbid.
523 if (cycle.size() < 2) return;
524
525 // Process each adjacent pair in the cycle.
526 for (size_t i = 0; i < cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
527 int v1 = cycle[i];
528 int v2 = cycle[i + 1];
529
530 // Forbid v2 in the neighborhood of v1.
531 size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(v2) >> 6;
532 size_t bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v2) & 63;
533 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] |= bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
534
535 if (aggressive) {
536 // Additionally, forbid v1 in the neighborhood of v2.
537 segment = static_cast<size_t>(v1) >> 6;
538 bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v1) & 63;
539 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] |= bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
540 }
541 }
542}

◆ generate_arcs() [1/2]

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in both forward and backward directions in parallel.

This function uses OpenMP to parallelize the generation of arcs in both forward and backward directions. It performs the following steps for each direction:

  • Calls the generate_arcs function template with the appropriate direction.
  • Clears and resizes the Phi vector for the respective direction.
  • Computes the Phi values for each bucket and stores them in the Phi vector.
  • Calls the SCC_handler function template with the appropriate direction.

The forward direction operations are performed in one OpenMP section, and the backward direction operations are performed in another OpenMP section.

Definition at line 883 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

883 {
884 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
885 work, bi_sched,
886 SECTION {
887 // Task for Forward Direction
888 generate_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
889 Phi_fw.clear();
890 Phi_fw.resize(fw_buckets_size);
891 for (int i = 0; i < fw_buckets_size; ++i) {
892 Phi_fw[i] = computePhi(i, true);
893 }
894 SCC_handler<Direction::Forward>();
895 },
896 SECTION {
897 // Task for Backward Direction
898 generate_arcs<Direction::Backward>();
899 Phi_bw.clear();
900 Phi_bw.resize(bw_buckets_size);
901 for (int i = 0; i < bw_buckets_size; ++i) {
902 Phi_bw[i] = computePhi(i, false);
903 }
904 SCC_handler<Direction::Backward>();
905 });
906}
