◆ generate_arcs() [2/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Definition at line 426 of file BucketUtils.h.

426 {
427 // Mutex for protecting global bucket updates.
428 std::mutex buckets_mutex;
429
430 // Clear the appropriate graph.
431 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
432 fw_bucket_graph.clear();
433 } else {
434 bw_bucket_graph.clear();
435 }
436
437 // Resolve references based on the direction.
438 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
439 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
440 auto &num_buckets_idx =
441 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
442 // Note: we no longer rely solely on bucket_splits here.
443
444 // Pre-compute intervals (base widths) for each node and each resource,
445 // using the same logic as in define_buckets.
446 const int num_resources = options.resources.size();
447 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_base_intervals(nodes.size());
448 // Also store the computed splits per resource for each node.
449 std::vector<std::vector<int>> node_split_counts(nodes.size());
450
451 for (size_t node_id = 0; node_id < nodes.size(); ++node_id) {
452 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
453 node_base_intervals[node_id].resize(num_resources);
454 node_split_counts[node_id].resize(num_resources);
455 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
456 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
457 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
458 int splits = 1;
459 if (std::fabs(full_range) >
460 std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
461 double splits_d =
462 (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
463 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
464 }
465 node_split_counts[node_id][r] = splits;
466 node_base_intervals[node_id][r] =
467 (node.ub[r] - node.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
468 }
469 }
470
471 // Clear all buckets and their arcs.
472 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
473 bucket.clear();
474 bucket.clear_arcs(D == Direction::Forward);
475 }
476
477 // Helper lambda: validate an arc and, if valid, add it.
478 auto try_add_arc =
479 [&](const VRPNode &curr_node, int from_bucket, const VRPNode &next_node,
480 int to_bucket, const std::vector<double> &curr_node_base,
481 const std::vector<double> &next_node_base,
482 const std::vector<double> &res_inc, double cost_inc) -> bool {
483 bool valid = true;
484 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
485 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
486 // For forward, check that adding the resource increment
487 // from the from-bucket does not push us past the next
488 // node's upper bound.
489 if (numericutils::gt(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r],
490 next_node.ub[r])) {
491 valid = false;
492 } else {
493 double max_calc =
494 std::max(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r],
495 next_node.lb[r]);
496 if (numericutils::lt(max_calc, buckets[to_bucket].lb[r]) ||
497 numericutils::gte(max_calc, buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] +
498 next_node_base[r])) {
499 valid = false;
500 }
501 }
502 }
503 } else {
504 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
505 // For backward, check that subtracting the resource
506 // increment does not drop below next node's lb.
507 if (numericutils::lt(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r],
508 next_node.lb[r])) {
509 valid = false;
510 } else {
511 double min_calc =
512 std::min(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r],
513 next_node.ub[r]);
514 if (numericutils::gt(min_calc, buckets[to_bucket].ub[r]) ||
515 numericutils::lte(min_calc, buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] -
516 next_node_base[r])) {
517 valid = false;
518 }
519 }
520 }
521 }
522 if (valid) {
523 // Add the arc to the global structure.
524 {
525 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(buckets_mutex);
526 add_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc);
527 buckets[from_bucket].template add_bucket_arc<D>(
528 from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc, false);
529 }
530 }
531 return valid;
532 };
533
534 // Lambda to process a single node.
535 auto process_node = [&](int node_id) {
536 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
537 std::vector<double> res_inc(num_resources);
538 // Local container for arcs (if further processing is needed).
539 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> local_arcs;
540 local_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
541
542 const auto arcs = node.get_arcs<D>();
543 // Use the pre-computed base intervals for the current node.
544 const auto &curr_node_base = node_base_intervals[node.id];
545
546 // For every bucket in the current node.
547 for (int i = 0; i < num_buckets[node.id]; ++i) {
548 int from_bucket = i + num_buckets_idx[node.id];
549 // Process each outgoing arc.
550 for (const auto &arc : nodes) {
551 const auto &next_node = arc;
552 // Avoid self-loop.
553 if (node.id == next_node.id) continue;
554
555 const auto &next_node_base = node_base_intervals[next_node.id];
556 const double travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
557 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
558 // Pre-calculate resource increments.
559 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; r++) {
560 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
561 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
562 res_inc[r] += travel_cost;
563 }
564 }
565
566 // Iterate over buckets in the next node.
567 for (int j = 0; j < num_buckets[next_node.id]; ++j) {
568 int to_bucket = j + num_buckets_idx[next_node.id];
569 // if (from_bucket == to_bucket ||
570 // is_bucket_fixed<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket)) {
571 // continue;
572 // }
573 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) {
574 continue;
575 }
576 // // Validate and add arc if valid.
577 if (try_add_arc(node, from_bucket, next_node, to_bucket,
578 curr_node_base, next_node_base, res_inc,
579 cost_inc)) {
580 local_arcs.emplace_back(from_bucket, to_bucket);
581 }
582 }
583 }
584 }
585 // If needed, local_arcs can be used for debugging or further
586 // processing.
587 };
588
589 // Create a list of tasks (each task is a node index).
590 std::vector<int> tasks(nodes.size());
591 std::iota(tasks.begin(), tasks.end(), 0);
592
593 // Choose a chunk size based on a heuristic.
594 constexpr int chunk_size = 10;
595
596 // Set up the thread pool.
597 const unsigned int thread_count =
598 std::max(1u, std::thread::hardware_concurrency() / 2);
599 exec::static_thread_pool pool(thread_count);
600 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
601
602 // Launch tasks in parallel over chunks.
603 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
604 stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size,
605 [&, chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
606 const size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
607 const size_t end_idx =
608 std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
609 for (size_t idx = start_idx; idx < end_idx; ++idx) {
610 process_node(tasks[idx]);
611 }
612 });
613
614 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
615 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
616}
pool
thread_local std::vector< std::vector< T > > ThreadLocalPool< T >::pool

◆ get_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
AdjList BucketGraph::get_adjacency_list ( )
inline

Definition at line 1306 of file BucketGraph.h.

1306 {
1307 AdjList adjacency_list;
1308
1309 // Iterate through all nodes in the graph
1310 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1311 std::vector<std::tuple<int, double, int>> arcs;
1312
1313 // Retrieve all arcs associated with the current node
1314 for (const auto &arc : node.get_arcs<Direction::Forward>()) {
1315 int to_node = arc.to;
1316 double cost = getcij(node.id, to_node) - nodes[to_node].cost;
1317 int capacity = 1;
1318
1319 // Only add arcs with non-zero capacity
1320 if (capacity > 0) {
1321 arcs.emplace_back(to_node, cost, capacity);
1322 }
1323 }
1324
1325 // Add the node's arcs to the adjacency list if it has
1326 // any outgoing edges
1327 if (!arcs.empty()) {
1328 adjacency_list[node.id] = arcs;
1329 }
1330 }
1331
1332 return adjacency_list;
1333 }

◆ get_best_label()

template<Direction D>
Label * BucketGraph::get_best_label ( const std::vector< int > & topological_order,
const std::vector< double > & c_bar,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > & sccs )

Retrieves the best label from the bucket graph based on the given topological order, c_bar values, and strongly connected components.

This function iterates through the given topological order and for each component, it retrieves the labels from the corresponding buckets in the bucket graph. It then compares the cost of each label and keeps track of the label with the lowest cost. The best label, along with its associated bucket, is returned.

Definition at line 630 of file BucketUtils.h.

632 {
633 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
634 Label *best_label = nullptr;
635 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
636
637 // Iterate over all buckets, and select the label with the lowest cost.
638 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
639 Label *label = bucket.get_best_label();
640 if (!label) continue;
641 if (label->cost < best_cost) {
642 best_cost = label->cost;
643 best_label = label;
644 }
645 }
646 return best_label;
647}

◆ get_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_bucket_number ( int node,
std::vector< double > & values )
inlinenoexcept

Definition at line 86 of file BucketUtils.h.

87 {
88 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
89
90 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
91
92 // Optionally adjust resource values (uncomment if you need epsilon
93 // adjustments)
94 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
95 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
96 // resource_values_vec[r] += numericutils::eps;
97 } else { // Direction::Backward
98 // resource_values_vec[r] -= numericutils::eps;
99 }
100 }
101 auto &num_buckets_index =
102 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
103 // Start with the offset for this node’s buckets.
104 int bucket_number = num_buckets_index[node];
105 int multiplier = 1;
106
107 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
108 // Node-specific resource bounds.
109 double node_lb = nodes[node].lb[r];
110 double node_ub = nodes[node].ub[r];
111
112 // Global bounds (if needed for computing splits).
113 double global_lb = R_min[r];
114 double global_ub = R_max[r];
115 double full_range = global_ub - global_lb;
116 double node_range = node_ub - node_lb;
117
118 // Determine the number of splits (i.e. intervals) for resource r.
119 int splits =
120 std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) *
121 intervals[r].interval)));
122 double interval_width = node_range / splits;
123
124 int pos = 0;
125 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
126 // Compute the bucket "digit" from the lower bound.
127 pos = std::min(
128 splits - 1,
129 static_cast<int>(std::floor((resource_values_vec[r] - node_lb) /
130 interval_width)));
131 } else { // Direction::Backward
132 // For backward, we consider the distance from the upper bound.
133 pos = std::min(
134 splits - 1,
135 static_cast<int>(std::floor((node_ub - resource_values_vec[r]) /
136 interval_width)));
137 }
138
139 bucket_number += pos * multiplier;
140 multiplier *= splits;
141 }
142
143 // check if buckets[bucket_number] contains the resource_vec
144 if (unlikely(!buckets[bucket_number].contains(resource_values_vec))) {
145 // print resource_values_vec
146 for (auto val : resource_values_vec) {
147 fmt::print("{}\n", val);
148 }
149 // print bucket bounds
150 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].lb[0]);
151 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].ub[0]);
152
153 std::throw_with_nested(
154 std::runtime_error("Resource values not contained in bucket"));
155 return -1;
156 }
157
158 return bucket_number;
159}

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 1021 of file BucketUtils.h.

1022 {
1023 // Retrieve the current bucket based on the current direction.
1024 const auto &current_bucket = (D == Direction::Forward)
1025 ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index]
1026 : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
1027 const int node = current_bucket.node_id;
1028 const auto &theNode = nodes[node];
1029
1030 // Pre-compute the reference point for the given node using the main
1031 // resources.
1032 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
1033 // Optionally, add an assertion or check that inc has the expected size.
1034 assert(inc.size() == num_resources && theNode.lb.size() == num_resources &&
1035 theNode.ub.size() == num_resources);
1036
1037 std::vector<double> reference_point(num_resources);
1038
1039 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1040 // For Forward direction:
1041 // Use the maximum of inc and the node's lower bounds.
1042 // This ensures we do not fall below the allowed lower bound.
1043 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
1044 reference_point[i] = std::max(inc[i], theNode.lb[i]);
1045 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
1046 // reference_point[i] += numericutils::eps;
1047 }
1048 // Get the opposite bucket using the Backward tree.
1049 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
1050 } else {
1051 // For Backward direction:
1052 // Use the minimum of inc and the node's upper bounds.
1053 // This ensures we do not exceed the allowed upper bound.
1054 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
1055 reference_point[i] = std::min(inc[i], theNode.ub[i]);
1056 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
1057 // reference_point[i] -= numericutils::eps;
1058 }
1059 // Get the opposite bucket using the Forward tree.
1060 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
1061 }
1062}

◆ heuristic_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

Definition at line 1143 of file BucketUtils.h.

1143 {
1144 if (fixed) {
1145 return;
1146 }
1147
1148 // Reset and initialize state.
1149 reset_pool();
1150 reset_fixed();
1151 common_initialization();
1152
1153 // Pre-allocate c-bar vectors with infinity.
1154 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(),
1155 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1156 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(),
1157 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1158
1159 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
1160
1161 // Run the appropriate labeling algorithms based on stage
1162 if (S == Stage::Three) {
1163 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Two with Full::Partial mode.
1164 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Two, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar,
1165 backward_cbar);
1166 } else if (S == Stage::Four) {
1167 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING ARC FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
1168 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
1169 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Four with Full::Full mode.
1170 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar,
1171 backward_cbar);
1172 }
1173
1174 // Prepare containers for grouping labels by node.
1175 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
1176
1177 // Preallocate vectors with nullptr to avoid resizing
1178 std::vector<const Label *> min_fw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1179 std::vector<const Label *> min_bw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1180
1181 // Find minimum cost label for each node directly from buckets
1182 // without creating intermediate label maps
1183 for (const auto &bucket : fw_buckets) {
1184 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
1185 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
1186 if (!min_fw_labels[node_id] ||
1187 label->cost < min_fw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
1188 min_fw_labels[node_id] = label;
1189 }
1190 }
1191 }
1192
1193 for (const auto &bucket : bw_buckets) {
1194 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
1195 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
1196 if (!min_bw_labels[node_id] ||
1197 label->cost < min_bw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
1198 min_bw_labels[node_id] = label;
1199 }
1200 }
1201 }
1202
1203 // Pre-compute the index of the "time" resource.
1204 const size_t time_index = std::distance(
1205 options.resources.begin(),
1206 std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time"));
1207
1208 // Precompute lookup tables for common values
1209 std::vector<double> fw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
1210 std::vector<double> bw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
1211 std::vector<double> fw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
1212 std::vector<double> bw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
1213
1214 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1215 if (min_fw_labels[i]) {
1216 fw_time_resources[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
1217 fw_costs[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->cost;
1218 }
1219 if (min_bw_labels[i]) {
1220 bw_time_resources[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
1221 bw_costs[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->cost;
1222 }
1223 }
1224
1225 // Cache node data to avoid repeated lookups
1226 std::vector<double> node_durations(num_nodes);
1227 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_consumptions(
1228 num_nodes, std::vector<double>(options.resources.size()));
1229
1230 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1231 const VRPNode &node = nodes[i];
1232 node_durations[i] = node.duration;
1233 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1234 node_consumptions[i][r] = node.consumption[r];
1235 }
1236 }
1237
1238 size_t num_fixes = 0;
1239 const double gap_threshold = gap; // Cache this value
1240
1241 // Build a list of candidate node pairs to check
1242 struct NodePair {
1243 size_t i;
1244 size_t j;
1245 };
1246
1247 std::vector<NodePair> candidate_pairs;
1248 candidate_pairs.reserve(num_nodes * num_nodes /
1249 4); // Estimate: 25% of pairs will be candidates
1250
1251 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1252 if (!min_fw_labels[i]) continue;
1253
1254 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
1255 if (i == j || !min_bw_labels[j]) continue;
1256 candidate_pairs.push_back({i, j});
1257 }
1258 }
1259
1260 // Process candidate pairs in batches to improve cache locality
1261 const size_t BATCH_SIZE = 256;
1262 std::vector<std::pair<size_t, size_t>> arcs_to_fix;
1263 arcs_to_fix.reserve(BATCH_SIZE);
1264
1265 for (size_t start = 0; start < candidate_pairs.size();
1266 start += BATCH_SIZE) {
1267 const size_t end = std::min(start + BATCH_SIZE, candidate_pairs.size());
1268 arcs_to_fix.clear();
1269
1270 for (size_t idx = start; idx < end; ++idx) {
1271 const auto &pair = candidate_pairs[idx];
1272 const size_t i = pair.i;
1273 const size_t j = pair.j;
1274
1275 const double cost =
1276 getcij(min_fw_labels[i]->node_id, min_bw_labels[j]->node_id);
1277
1278 // Early rejection using precomputed time resources (often most
1279 // restrictive)
1280 if (options.resources[time_index] == "time") {
1281 if (fw_time_resources[i] + cost +
1282 node_durations[min_fw_labels[i]->node_id] >
1283 bw_time_resources[j]) {
1284 continue;
1285 }
1286 }
1287
1288 // Check all other resources
1289 bool violated = false;
1290 const size_t fw_node_id = min_fw_labels[i]->node_id;
1291
1292 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1293 if (r != time_index) {
1294 if (min_fw_labels[i]->resources[r] +
1295 node_consumptions[fw_node_id][r] >
1296 min_bw_labels[j]->resources[r]) {
1297 violated = true;
1298 break;
1299 }
1300 }
1301 }
1302
1303 // If all resource constraints are satisfied and the cost exceeds
1304 // the gap, fix the arc
1305 if (!violated &&
1306 (fw_costs[i] + cost + bw_costs[j] > gap_threshold)) {
1307 arcs_to_fix.emplace_back(i, j);
1308 ++num_fixes;
1309 }
1310 }
1311
1312 // Fix arcs in batch
1313 for (const auto &[i, j] : arcs_to_fix) {
1314 fix_arc(i, j);
1315 }
1316 }
1317
1318 if (S == Stage::Four) {
1319 fmt::print("Applied {} arc fixes\n", num_fixes);
1320 fmt::print("\033[34m_ARC FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
1321 }
1322}

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 988 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

988 {
989 // Print header
990 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
991 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
992 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
993
994 // Print Resource size
995 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
996 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
997
998 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
999#ifdef RIH
1000 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
1001#else
1002 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
1003#endif
1004#ifdef RCC
1005 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
1006#else
1007 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
1008#endif
1009#ifdef SRC
1010 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
1011#else
1012 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
1013#endif
1014 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
1015
1016 fmt::print("\n");
1017}

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::is_dominated ( const Label *__restrict new_label,
const Label *__restrict label )
inlinenoexcept

Checks if a label is dominated by a new label based on cost and resource conditions.

Definition at line 928 of file BucketSolve.h.

929 {
930 // A label cannot dominate if its cost is higher.
931 // const double cost_diff = label->cost - new_label->cost;
932 if (numericutils::gt(label->cost, new_label->cost)) {
933 return false;
934 }
935
936 // Compare resource vectors.
937 const auto *__restrict new_res = new_label->resources.data();
938 const auto *__restrict lbl_res = label->resources.data();
939 const size_t n_res = options.resources.size();
940
941 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
942 // In the Forward direction, each resource value of 'label' must
943 // not exceed that of 'new_label'
944 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_res; ++i) {
945 if (numericutils::gt(lbl_res[i], new_res[i])) {
946 return false;
947 }
948 }
949 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
950 // In the Backward direction, each resource value of 'label'
951 // must not be less than that of 'new_label'
952 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_res; ++i) {
953 if (numericutils::lt(lbl_res[i], new_res[i])) {
954 return false;
955 }
956 }
957 }
958
959 // visits of label.
960 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four ||
961 S == Stage::Enumerate) {
962 const size_t n_bitmap = label->visited_bitmap.size();
963 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_bitmap; ++i) {
964 // Every visited node in 'label' must also be visited in
965 // 'new_label'.
966 if ((label->visited_bitmap[i] & new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) !=
967 label->visited_bitmap[i]) {
968 return false;
969 }
970 }
971 }
972
973#ifdef SRC
974 // For Stage Four or Enumerate, apply additional SRC-based cost
975 // adjustments.
976 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
977 const double local_cost_diff = label->cost - new_label->cost;
978 const auto *__restrict lbl_srcs = label->SRCmap.data();
979 const auto *__restrict new_lbl_srcs = new_label->SRCmap.data();
980 const auto &active_cuts = cut_storage->getActiveCuts();
981 double dual_sum = 0.0;
982 const size_t n = active_cuts.size();
983 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
984 const auto &cut = active_cuts[i];
985 // Branch prediction hint: likely false.
986 if (__builtin_expect(lbl_srcs[cut.index] > new_lbl_srcs[cut.index],
987 0)) {
988 dual_sum += cut.dual_value;
989 if (__builtin_expect(
990 (numericutils::gt(local_cost_diff, dual_sum)), 0)) {
991 return false;
992 }
993 }
994 }
995 }
996#endif
997
998 return true;
999}

◆ is_node_unreachable()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_unreachable ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

Definition at line 592 of file BucketGraph.h.

593 {
594 int word_index = node_id >> 6; // Determine which 64-bit segment

597 node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position within that segment
598 return (bitmap[word_index] & (1ULL << bit_position)) != 0;
599 }

◆ is_node_visited()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.

double BucketGraph::knapsackBound ( const Label * l)

◆ labeling_algorithm()

template<Direction D, Stage S, Full F>
std::vector< double > BucketGraph::labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

Definition at line 206 of file BucketSolve.h.

206 {
207 // Cache bucket and auxiliary structure references to avoid repeated
208 // lookups.
209 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
210 const auto &ordered_sccs =
211 assign_buckets<D>(fw_ordered_sccs, bw_ordered_sccs);
212 const auto &topological_order =
213 assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
214 const auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs, bw_sccs);
215 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
216 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
217 auto &n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(n_fw_labels, n_bw_labels);
218 const auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
219 const auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
220 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
221 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
222 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
223 auto &best_label = assign_buckets<D>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
224
225 // Reset the total label count.
226 n_labels = 0;
227
228 // Pre-calculate bitmap segments for dominance-checking.
229 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
230 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
231
232 // Preallocate vector for extended labels to avoid repeated allocations.
233 std::vector<Label *> extended_labels;
234 extended_labels.reserve(64);
235
236 // Precompute main resource index and q_star value for partial solutions.
237 constexpr bool is_partial = (F == Full::Partial);
238 int main_res_index = 0;
239 double q_star_value = 0.0;
240 if constexpr (is_partial) {
241 main_res_index = options.main_resources[0];
242 q_star_value = std::floor(q_star[main_res_index]);
243 }
244
245 // Reuse a thread-local vector for collecting labels to dominate.
246 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> tl_labels_to_dominate;
247 tl_labels_to_dominate.clear();
248
249 // Process each strongly connected component (SCC) in topological order.
250 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
251 bool all_ext = false;
252 do {
253 all_ext = true; // Assume no new extensions until one occurs.
254 const auto &scc_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
255 for (const auto bucket : scc_buckets) {
256 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_labels();
257 for (Label *label : bucket_labels) {
258 // Skip labels already processed.
259 if (label->is_extended || label->is_dominated) continue;
260
261 // For partial solutions, check resource feasibility.
262 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
263 if constexpr (is_partial) {
264 const auto main_resource =
265 label->resources[main_res_index];
266 if ((D == Direction::Forward &&
267 main_resource > q_star_value) ||
268 (D == Direction::Backward &&
269 main_resource <= q_star_value)) {
270 label->set_extended(true);
271 continue;
272 }
273 }
274 }
275
276 // Clear the bitmap using memset (faster than std::fill for
277 // large arrays).
278 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
279 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
280
281 // Check if the label is dominated by labels in smaller
282 // buckets.
283 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
284 if (DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(
285 label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, stat_n_dom))
286 continue;
287 }
288
289 // Retrieve outgoing arcs for the current label.
290 const auto &node_arcs =
291 buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
292 if (node_arcs.empty()) {
293 label->set_extended(true);
294 continue;
295 }
296
297 for (const auto &arc : node_arcs) {
298 extended_labels.clear();
299 extended_labels =
300 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Bucket, Mutability::Mut, F>(
301 label, arc, &extended_labels);
302
303 // Process each new label produced by the extension.
304 for (Label *new_label : extended_labels) {
305 ++stat_n_labels;
306 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
307 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
308 const auto &to_bucket_labels =
309 mother_bucket.get_labels();
310
311 bool dominated = false;
312 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
313 // Stage One: mark higher-cost labels as
314 // dominated.
315 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
316 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
317 if (label->cost < existing_label->cost)
318 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
319 else {
320 dominated = true;
321 break;
322 }
323 }
324 } else {
325 // Later stages: use a SIMD-friendly, unrolled
326 // loop.
327 auto check_dominance_in_bucket =
328 [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels,
329 int &stat_n_dom) -> bool {
330#ifdef __AVX2__
331 if (labels.size() >= AVX_LIM)
332 return check_dominance_against_vector<
333 D, S>(new_label, labels,
334 cut_storage,
335 options.resources.size(),
336 stat_n_dom);
337 else
338#endif
339 {
340 const size_t size = labels.size();
341 size_t i = 0;
342 for (; i + 3 < size; i += 4) {
343 if ((!labels[i]->is_dominated &&
344 is_dominated<D, S>(
345 new_label, labels[i])) ||
346 (!labels[i + 1]->is_dominated &&
347 is_dominated<D, S>(
348 new_label,
349 labels[i + 1])) ||
350 (!labels[i + 2]->is_dominated &&
351 is_dominated<D, S>(
352 new_label,
353 labels[i + 2])) ||
354 (!labels[i + 3]->is_dominated &&
355 is_dominated<D, S>(
356 new_label,
357 labels[i + 3]))) {
358 ++stat_n_dom;
359 return true;
360 }
361 }
362 for (; i < size; ++i) {
363 if (!labels[i]->is_dominated &&
364 is_dominated<D, S>(new_label,
365 labels[i])) {
366 ++stat_n_dom;
367 return true;
368 }
369 }
370 return false;
371 }
372 };
373 dominated = mother_bucket.check_dominance(
374 new_label, check_dominance_in_bucket,
375 stat_n_dom);
376 }
377
378 if (!dominated) {
379 // Reuse thread-local vector to avoid
380 // re-allocation.
381 tl_labels_to_dominate.clear();
382 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
383 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
384 if (is_dominated<D, S>(existing_label,
385 new_label))
386 tl_labels_to_dominate.push_back(
387 existing_label);
388 }
389 for (auto *existing_label :
390 tl_labels_to_dominate)
391 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
392
393 ++n_labels;
394#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
395 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
396#elif defined(LIMITED_BUCKETS)
397 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label,
398 BUCKET_CAPACITY);
399#else
400 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
401#endif
402 // Mark that a new extension was added.
403 all_ext = false;
404 }
405 }
406 }
407 label->set_extended(true);
408 }
409 }
410 } while (!all_ext);
411
412 // Update c_bar values for all buckets in the current SCC.
413 auto sorted_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
414 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
415 pdqsort(sorted_buckets.begin(), sorted_buckets.end(),
416 [&](int a, int b) {
417 return buckets[a].get_lb() < buckets[b].get_lb();
418 });
419 } else {
420 pdqsort(sorted_buckets.begin(), sorted_buckets.end(),
421 [&](int a, int b) {
422 return buckets[a].get_ub() > buckets[b].get_ub();
423 });
424 }
425 // Batch processing of c_bar updates for improved cache locality.
426 for (const int bucket : sorted_buckets) {
427 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
428 const auto &phi_buckets = Phi[bucket];
429 for (const auto phi_bucket : phi_buckets)
430 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_bucket]);
431 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
432 }
433 }
434
435 // Store the best label from the labeling process.
436 if (Label *best_label_run =
437 get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs))
438 best_label = best_label_run;
439
440 return c_bar;
441}
Stage::One

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

Definition at line 681 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

681 {
682 // Clear previous data
683 merged_labels.clear();
684 merged_labels.reserve(100);
685 fw_c_bar.clear();
686 bw_c_bar.clear();
687
688 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
689 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
690
691 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
692 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
693 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
694
695 auto &num_buckets =
696 assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
697 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(
698 num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
699
700 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
701 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
702 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
703
704 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
705
706 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
707 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
708 base_intervals[r] =
709 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
710 }
711
712 // Clear forward and backward buckets
713 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
714 fw_buckets[b].clear();
715 bw_buckets[b].clear();
716 }
717
718 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
719 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
720
721 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
722 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
723
724 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper
725 // bounds
726 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
727 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
728 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
729 }
730 int offset_fw = 0;
731 int offset_bw = 0;
732
733 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across
734 // combinations
735 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
736 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
737 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
738 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
739 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
740 // print depot_id
741 int calculated_index_base =
742 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
743 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
744
745 std::vector<int> current_pos(
746 num_intervals,
747 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
748 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
749
750 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
751 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
752 if (depth == num_intervals) {
753 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current
754 // combination
755 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
756 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
757
758 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval
759 // starts or ends
760 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
761 interval_bounds[r] =
762 is_forward
763 ? interval_starts[r] +
764 current_pos[r] *
765 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
766 : interval_ends[r] -
767 current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(
768 base_intervals[r]);
769 }
770
771 // print interval
772 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
773 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
774 depot_id);
775 depot->is_extended = false;
776 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
777 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
778 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
779 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
780 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
781 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
782 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
783
784 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
785 return;
786 }
787
788 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
789 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
790 current_pos[depth] = k;
791 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
792 }
793 };
794
795 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
796 generate_combinations(0);
797 };
798
799 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring
800 // all combinations are processed
801 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
802}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 391 of file BucketJump.h.

391 {
392 // Select direction-specific containers.
393 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
394 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
395 auto &num_buckets_idx =
396 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
397 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
398 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
399 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
400
401 int arc_counter = 0; // Count of jump arcs added
402 int missing_counter = 0; // Count of missing paths
403
404 // Clear jump arcs for all nodes.
405 for (auto &node : nodes) {
406 node.clear_jump_arcs<D>();
407 }
408
409 // Process each bucket.
410 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
411 std::vector<int> B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid adjacent
412 // bucket indices
413
414 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
415 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
416 const auto &orig_arcs = nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
417
418 // Skip if there are no bucket arcs.
419 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) {
420 continue;
421 }
422
423 // For each original arc from the current node...
424 for (const auto &orig_arc : orig_arcs) {
425 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
426 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
427 bool have_path = false;
428
429 // Check if the current bucket arcs contain the desired
430 // path.
431 for (const auto &gamma : bucket_arcs) {
432 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma.from_bucket, gamma.to_bucket)) {
433 continue;
434 }
435
436 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
437 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
438
439 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
440 have_path = true;
441 break; // Path found: no need to search further.
442 }
443 }
444
445 // If no valid path was found, try to obtain a jump arc.
446 if (!have_path) {
447 ++missing_counter;
448
449 // Determine bucket range for the current node.
450 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_idx[current_node_id];
451 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
452
453 // Lambda to process candidate adjacent buckets.
454 auto process_bucket = [&](int current_b, int candidate_b) {
455 // Only proceed if candidate bucket is adjacent (per
456 // Phi).
457 const auto &phi_candidate = Phi[candidate_b];
458 if (std::find(phi_candidate.begin(), phi_candidate.end(),
459 current_b) == phi_candidate.end())
460 return;
461
462 // Check candidate bucket's arcs.
463 const auto &candidate_arcs =
464 buckets[candidate_b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
465 for (const auto &gamma_candidate : candidate_arcs) {
466 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma_candidate.from_bucket,
467 gamma_candidate.to_bucket))
468 continue;
469
470 const int from_node_candidate =
471 buckets[gamma_candidate.from_bucket].node_id;
472 const int to_node_candidate =
473 buckets[gamma_candidate.to_bucket].node_id;
474
475 // If the candidate arc matches the original
476 // arc...
477 if (from_node == from_node_candidate &&
478 to_node == to_node_candidate) {
479 B_bar.push_back(candidate_b);
480 std::vector<double> res =
481 gamma_candidate.resource_increment;
482
483 const double cost = gamma_candidate.cost_increment;
484
485 // Add jump arcs both in the bucket and at
486 // the node level.
487 buckets[current_b].template add_jump_arc<D>(
488 current_b, candidate_b, res, cost);
489 nodes[buckets[current_b].node_id]
490 .template add_jump_arc<D>(
491 current_b, candidate_b, res, cost, to_node);
492 buckets[current_b].template add_bucket_arc<D>(
493 current_b, candidate_b, res, cost, true);
494 ++arc_counter;
495 }
496 }
497 };
498
499 // Process candidate buckets. For now both directions
500 // use increasing index. (For backward direction, adjust
501 // the loop as needed.)
502 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
503 for (int candidate_b = b + 1;
504 candidate_b < start_bucket + node_buckets;
505 ++candidate_b) {
506 process_bucket(b, candidate_b);
507 }
508 } else {
509 // Uncomment and adjust if backward should iterate
510 // in reverse order.
511 // for (int candidate_b = b - 1; candidate_b >=
512 // start_bucket;
513 // --candidate_b) {
514 // process_bucket(b, candidate_b);
515 // }
516 for (int candidate_b = b + 1;
517 candidate_b < start_bucket + node_buckets;
518 ++candidate_b) {
519 process_bucket(b, candidate_b);
520 }
521 }
522 }
523 }
524 }
525
526 // Print summary of jump arcs added.
527 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
528 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
529}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 839 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

839 {
840 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
841 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
842 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
843
844 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
845 fmt::print(
846 "\n+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
847 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset,
848 " Forward", " Backward");
849 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
850
851 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
852 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset,
853 stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
854
855 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
856 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold,
857 " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
858
859 // Print the final horizontal line
860 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
861
862 fmt::print("\n");
863}
reset
constexpr auto reset

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 114 of file BucketRes.h.

117 {
118 for (size_t I = 0; I < N; ++I) {
119 switch (static_cast<ResourceType>(options.resource_type[I])) {
120 [[likely]] case ResourceType::Disposable:
121 if (!process_disposable_resource<D>(
122 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
123 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.lb[I],
124 theNode.ub[I])) {
125 return false;
126 }
127 break;
128
129 case ResourceType::NonDisposable:
130 if (!process_non_disposable_resource<D>(
131 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
132 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.lb[I],
133 theNode.ub[I])) {
134 return false;
135 }
136 break;
137
138 case ResourceType::Binary:
139 new_resources[I] =
140 process_binary_resource<D>(gamma.resource_increment[I]);
141 break;
142
143 case ResourceType::Battery:
144 if (!process_battery_resource<D>(
145 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
146 theNode.is_station, options.battery_capacity)) {
147 return false;
148 }
149 break;
150
151 case ResourceType::RechargeCount:
152 if (!process_recharge_count<D>(
153 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
154 theNode.is_station, options.max_recharges)) {
155 return false;
156 }
157 break;
158
159 case ResourceType::RechargeTime:
160 if (!process_recharge_time<D>(
161 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
162 theNode.is_station, options.max_recharge_time)) {
163 return false;
164 }
165 break;
166
167 case ResourceType::MTW:
168 if (!process_mtw_resource<D>(
169 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
170 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode)) {
171 return false;
172 }
173 break;
174 }
175 }
176 return true;
177}

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

◆ prune_ng_cycles()

void BucketGraph::prune_ng_cycles ( int max_age,
int min_usage,
int current_iteration )

Definition at line 331 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

332 {
333 for (auto it = ng_cycles.begin(); it != ng_cycles.end();) {
334 if (current_iteration - it->last_used_iteration > max_age &&
335 it->usage_count < min_usage) {
336 // Remove the corresponding forbidden edges from the
337 // neighborhoods_bitmap.
338 for (size_t i = 0; i < it->cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
339 int v1 = it->cycle[i];
340 int v2 = it->cycle[i + 1];
341 // Clear the bit corresponding to v2 in the neighborhood of
342 // v1.
343 size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(v2) >> 6;
344 size_t bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v2) & 63;
345 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] &=
346 ~bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
347
348 // If your approach was aggressive, also clear the reverse.
349 segment = static_cast<size_t>(v1) >> 6;
350 bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v1) & 63;
351 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] &=
352 ~bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
353 }
354 it = ng_cycles.erase(it);
355 } else {
356 ++it;
357 }
358 }
359}

◆ redefine()

void BucketGraph::redefine ( int bucketInterval)
inline

Redefines the bucket intervals and reinitializes various data structures.

This function updates the bucket interval and reinitializes the intervals, buckets, fixed arcs, and fixed buckets. It also generates arcs and sorts them for each node.

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

Definition at line 823 of file BucketGraph.h.

823 {
824 // for (auto &row : fixed_arcs) {
825 // std::fill(row.begin(), row.end(), 0);
826 // }
827 size_t bitmap_size = (nodes.size() * nodes.size() + 63) / 64;
828 fixed_arcs_bitmap.assign(bitmap_size, 0);
829 }

◆ reset_pool()

void BucketGraph::reset_pool ( )
inline

Resets the forward and backward label pools.

This function resets both the forward (label_pool_fw) and backward (label_pool_bw) label pools to their initial states. It is typically used to clear any existing labels and prepare the pools for reuse.

Definition at line 782 of file BucketGraph.h.

782 {
783 label_pool_fw->reset();
784 label_pool_bw->reset();
785 }

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

T#his function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1149 of file BucketSolve.h.

1150 {
1151 // Schedule the forward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1152 auto forward_task =
1153 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1154 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>();
1155 });
1156
1157 // Schedule the backward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1158 auto backward_task =
1159 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1160 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>();
1161 });
1162
1163 // Combine the forward and backward tasks.
1164 auto combined_work =
1165 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1166 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1167 // Store the results into the provided vectors.
1168 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1169 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1170 });
1171
1172 // Wait for all tasks to complete.
1173 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(combined_work));
1174}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ SCC_handler()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::SCC_handler ( )

Handles the computation of Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) for the BucketGraph.

This function processes the bucket graph to identify SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm. It extends the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets, computes the SCCs, and orders them topologically. It also sorts the buckets within each SCC based on their lower or upper bounds, depending on the direction. Finally, it splits the arcs for each SCC and removes duplicates.

Definition at line 864 of file BucketUtils.h.

864 {
865 // Get necessary references.
866 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
867 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
868 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
869
870 // Build an extended bucket graph from bucket_graph using Phi.
871 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph =
872 bucket_graph;
873 for (size_t i = 0; i < extended_bucket_graph.size(); ++i) {
874 const auto &phi_set = Phi[i];
875 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
876 for (auto phi_bucket : phi_set) {
877 extended_bucket_graph[phi_bucket].push_back(i);
878 }
879 }
880
881 // Compute SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm.
882 SCC scc_finder;
883 scc_finder.convertFromUnorderedMap(extended_bucket_graph);
884 auto sccs = scc_finder.tarjanSCC();
885 auto topological_order = scc_finder.topologicalOrderOfSCCs(sccs);
886
887#ifdef VERBOSE
888 // Optional: Print SCCs.
889 constexpr auto blue = "\033[34m";
890 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
891 fmt::print((D == Direction::Forward) ? "FW SCCs:\n" : "BW SCCs:\n");
892 for (auto scc : topological_order) {
893 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
894 for (auto bucket : sccs[scc]) {
895 fmt::print("{} ", bucket);
896 }
897 fmt::print("\n");
898 }
899 fmt::print("\n");
900#endif
901
902 // Order SCCs according to the topological order.
903 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
904 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size());
905 for (int scc : topological_order) {
906 ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[scc]);
907 }
908
909 // Sort each SCC: For Forward, sort by bucket lower bound; for Backward,
910 // sort by bucket upper bound.
911 auto sorted_sccs = sccs; // Make a copy
912 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
913 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
914 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
915 return buckets[a].lb[0] < buckets[b].lb[0];
916 });
917 } else {
918 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
919 return buckets[a].ub[0] > buckets[b].ub[0];
920 });
921 }
922 }
923
924 // Ensure each VRP node has the appropriate SCC arc container allocated.
925 for (auto &node : nodes) {
926 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
927 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
928 } else {
929 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
930 }
931 }
932
933 // Lambda: Filter and collect arcs for a given bucket, node, and
934 // direction.
935 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int bucket, int scc_index) {
936 int from_node_id = buckets[bucket].node_id;
937 VRPNode &node = nodes[from_node_id];
938 // Select the proper arc container and node arcs based on the
939 // direction.
940 const auto &node_arcs =
941 (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs : node.bw_arcs;
942 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_arcs = (D == Direction::Forward)
943 ? node.fw_arcs_scc[scc_index]
944 : node.bw_arcs_scc[scc_index];
945
946 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
947 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
948 int to_node_id = buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id;
949 // Look for an arc with destination equal to to_node_id.
950 auto it = std::find_if(
951 node_arcs.begin(), node_arcs.end(),
952 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) { return a.to == to_node_id; });
953 if (it != node_arcs.end()) {
954 filtered_arcs.push_back(*it);
955 }
956 }
957 };
958
959 // For each SCC, process arcs for each bucket in the SCC.
960 int scc_ctr = 0;
961 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
962 for (int bucket : scc) {
963 process_bucket_arcs(bucket, scc_ctr);
964 }
965 ++scc_ctr;
966 }
967
968 // Lambda: Sort and deduplicate arcs within each node's SCC arc
969 // container.
970 auto sortAndDedup = [](std::vector<Arc> &arcs) {
971 pdqsort(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(), [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
972 return std::tie(a.from, a.to) < std::tie(b.from, b.to);
973 });
974 auto last = std::unique(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
975 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
976 return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to;
977 });
978 arcs.erase(last, arcs.end());
979 };
980
981 // For each node, sort and deduplicate all arc lists.
982 for (auto &node : nodes) {
983 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
984 for (auto &arcs : node.fw_arcs_scc) {
985 sortAndDedup(arcs);
986 }
987 } else {
988 for (auto &arcs : node.bw_arcs_scc) {
989 sortAndDedup(arcs);
990 }
991 }
992 }
993
994 // Build union-find structure for SCCs using the ordered SCCs.
995 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
996 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
997 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
998 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
999 fw_sccs = sccs;
1000 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
1001 fw_union_find = uf;
1002 } else {
1003 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
1004 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
1005 bw_sccs = sccs;
1006 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
1007 bw_union_find = uf;
1008 }
1009}

◆ set_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 1325 of file BucketUtils.h.

1325 {
1326 // === Step 1: Clear Existing Arcs from Each Node ===
1327 for (auto &node : nodes) {
1328 node.clear_arcs();
1329 }
1330
1331 // === Step 2: MST-Based Clustering ===
1332 MST mst_solver(nodes,
1333 [this](int from, int to) { return getcij(from, to); });
1334 auto mst = mst_solver.compute_mst();
1335
1336 // Collect and sort MST edge weights.
1337 std::vector<double> edge_weights;
1338 edge_weights.reserve(mst.size());
1339 for (const auto &[weight, from, to] : mst) {
1340 edge_weights.push_back(weight);
1341 }
1342 pdqsort(edge_weights.begin(), edge_weights.end());
1343
1344 // Compute theta as the 90th percentile (scaled down by 100).
1345 size_t p90_index = static_cast<size_t>(0.9 * edge_weights.size());
1346 double theta = (edge_weights[p90_index]) / 100.0;
1347 print_info("Computed theta: {}\n", theta);
1348
1349 // Cluster nodes and build mapping: job_id -> cluster_id.
1350 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
1351 print_info("Number of clusters: {}\n", clusters.size());
1352 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1);
1353 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
1354 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
1355 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
1356 }
1357 }
1358
1359 // === Step 3: Precompute Resource Information ===
1360 // Find the index for the "time" resource.
1361 auto time_it =
1362 std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time");
1363 const int time_resource_idx =
1364 (time_it != options.resources.end())
1365 ? std::distance(options.resources.begin(), time_it)
1366 : -1;
1367
1368 // === Step 4: Process Arcs for Each Node ===
1369
1370 // Helper lambda to compute resource increments.
1371 // Depending on symmetry, consumption is either taken as-is or averaged.
1372 auto compute_resource_increments = [&](const VRPNode &from_node,
1373 const VRPNode &to_node,
1374 double travel_cost,
1375 std::vector<double> &inc) {
1376 const int nres = options.resources.size();
1377 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
1378 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
1379 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx)
1380 ? travel_cost + from_node.duration
1381 : from_node.consumption[r];
1382 }
1383 } else {
1384 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
1385 inc[r] =
1386 (r == time_resource_idx)
1387 ? (from_node.duration / 2.0 + travel_cost +
1388 to_node.duration / 2.0)
1389 : ((from_node.consumption[r] + to_node.consumption[r]) /
1390 2.0);
1391 }
1392 }
1393 };
1394
1395 // Lambda to add forward and reverse arcs from a given node.
1396 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &curr_node) {
1397 // Containers to accumulate arcs before insertion.
1398 using ArcTuple = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>, double>;
1399 std::vector<ArcTuple> forward_arcs;
1400 std::vector<ArcTuple> reverse_arcs;
1401 forward_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1402 reverse_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1403
1404 // Iterate over candidate next nodes.
1405 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
1406 // Skip if the candidate is a depot, the same node, or a pstep
1407 // depot.
1408 if (next_node.id == options.depot || curr_node.id == next_node.id ||
1409 (options.pstep && (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot ||
1410 next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot))) {
1411 continue;
1412 }
1413
1414 double travel_cost = getcij(curr_node.id, next_node.id);
1415 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
1416
1417 // Skip self-loops (or if bucket indexes match; here using node
1418 // id as bucket id).
1419 if (curr_node.id == next_node.id) continue;
1420
1421 // Compute resource increments.
1422 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
1423 compute_resource_increments(curr_node, next_node, travel_cost,
1424 res_inc);
1425
1426 // Check feasibility: ensure resource consumption does not
1427 // exceed next node's upper bound.
1428 bool feasible = true;
1429 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1430 if (numericutils::gt(curr_node.lb[r] + res_inc[r],
1431 next_node.ub[r])) {
1432 feasible = false;
1433 break;
1434 }
1435 }
1436 if (!feasible) continue;
1437
1438 // Compute priorities based on cluster membership.
1439 bool same_cluster =
1440 (job_to_cluster[curr_node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]);
1441 double forward_priority =
1442 (same_cluster ? 5.0 : 1.0) + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time;
1443 double reverse_priority =
1444 (same_cluster ? 1.0 : 5.0) + 1.E-5 * curr_node.start_time;
1445
1446 // Collect arcs for forward and reverse directions.
1447 forward_arcs.emplace_back(forward_priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1448 cost_inc);
1449 reverse_arcs.emplace_back(reverse_priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1450 cost_inc);
1451 }
1452
1453 // Insert the arcs into the corresponding nodes.
1454 for (const auto &[priority, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc] :
1455 forward_arcs) {
1456 nodes[curr_node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
1457 curr_node.id, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc, priority);
1458 }
1459 for (const auto &[priority, from_node, res_inc, cost_inc] :
1460 reverse_arcs) {
1461 nodes[from_node].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
1462 from_node, curr_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc, priority);
1463 }
1464 };
1465
1466 // Process each node, skipping the end depot.
1467 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1468 if (node.id != options.end_depot) {
1469 add_arcs_for_node(node);
1470 }
1471 }
1472}

void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list_manual ( )

Definition at line 544 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

544 {
545 // Clear existing arcs for each node
546 for (auto &node : nodes) {
547 node.clear_arcs(); // Remove any existing arcs associated with the
548 // node
549 }
550
551 // Step 1: Compute the clusters using MST-based clustering
552 MST mst_solver(nodes,
553 [&](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
554 double theta = 1.0; // Experiment with different values of θ
555 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
556
557 // Create a job-to-cluster mapping (cluster ID for each job/node)
558 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(
559 nodes.size(), -1); // Mapping from job (node) to cluster ID
560 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
561 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
562 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
563 }
564 }
565
566 // Step 2: Modify add_arcs_for_node to give priority based on cluster
567 // membership
568 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
569 std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
570 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>,
571 double>; // Arc: priority, to_node, resource
572 // increments, cost increment
573
574 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs;
575 best_arcs.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for forward arcs
576
577 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs_rev;
578 best_arcs_rev.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for reverse arcs
579
580 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
581 if (!manual_arcs.has_arc(node.id, next_node.id))
582 continue; // Skip arcs not in the manual list
583
584 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id)
585 continue; // Skip depot and same node
586
587 auto travel_cost =
588 getcij(node.id, next_node.id); // Calculate travel cost
589 double cost_inc =
590 travel_cost - next_node.cost; // Adjust cost increment by
591 // subtracting next node's cost
592
593 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
594 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
595 res_inc[r] = travel_cost +
596 node.duration; // Update resource increment
597 // based on node duration
598 } else {
599 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
600 }
601 }
602 // res_inc[TIME_INDEX] += travel_cost; // Add travel time to
603 // resource increment
604
605 int to_bucket = next_node.id;
606 if (from_bucket == to_bucket)
607 continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
608
609 bool feasible =
610 true; // Check feasibility based on resource constraints
611 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
612 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
613 feasible = false;
614 break;
615 }
616 }
617 if (!feasible) continue; // Skip infeasible arcs
618
619 // Step 3: Calculate priority based on cluster membership
620 double priority_value;
621 double reverse_priority_value;
622 if (job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]) {
623 // Higher priority if both nodes are in the same cluster
624 priority_value =
625 5.0 +
626 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
627 // same-cluster priority
628 reverse_priority_value =
629 1.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
630 // same-cluster priority
631 } else {
632 // Lower priority for cross-cluster arcs
633 priority_value =
634 1.0 +
635 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Higher base value for
636 // cross-cluster arcs
637 reverse_priority_value =
638 5.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Higher base value for
639 // cross-cluster arcs
640 }
641
642 best_arcs.emplace_back(priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
643 cost_inc); // Store the forward arc
644 best_arcs_rev.emplace_back(reverse_priority_value, next_node.id,
645 res_inc,
646 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
647 }
648
649 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors
650 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs) {
651 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
652 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
653 node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
654 priority_value); // Add forward arc
655 }
656
657 // Add reverse arcs from neighboring nodes to the current node
658 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs_rev) {
659 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
660 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
661 to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
662 priority_value); // Add reverse arc
663 }
664 };
665
666 // Step 4: Iterate over all nodes to set the adjacency list
667 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
668 if (VRPNode.id == options.end_depot)
669 continue; // Skip the last node (depot)
670
671 std::vector<double> res_inc(
672 intervals.size()); // Resource increment vector
673 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, VRPNode.id,
674 res_inc); // Add arcs for the current node
675 }
676}

◆ set_distance_matrix()

void BucketGraph::set_distance_matrix ( const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & distanceMatrix,

Marks a node as unreachable in the given bitmap.

This function sets the bit corresponding to the specified node_id in the bitmap, indicating that the node is unreachable.

void BucketGraph::setDuals ( const std::vector< double > & duals)

Sets the dual values for the nodes.

◆ shallUpdateStep()

bool BucketGraph::shallUpdateStep ( )
inline

Definition at line 1335 of file BucketGraph.h.

1335 {
1336 int update_ctr = 0;
1337 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int> updated_buckets;
1338
1339 // Iterate over all buckets and check if either forward or backward
1340 // bucket indicates a split is needed.
1341 for (size_t b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); ++b) {
1342 if (fw_buckets[b].shall_split || bw_buckets[b].shall_split) {
1343 const int inner_id = fw_buckets[b].node_id;
1344 // Skip depot buckets.
1345 if (inner_id == options.depot ||
1346 inner_id == options.end_depot) {
1347 continue;
1348 }
1349 // If this node was already updated, skip it.
1350 if (updated_buckets.contains(inner_id)) {
1351 continue;
1352 }
1353 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1354 // If the bucket split value is already at maximum (100),
1355 // skip.(127)
1356 //
1357 if (fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] == 100) {
1358 continue;
1359 }
1360 // Update the bucket splits by doubling the current value,
1361 // capped at 100.
1362 const int update_target =
1363 std::min(fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] * 2, 100);
1364 fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1365 bw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1366 updated_buckets.insert(inner_id);
1367 }
1368 update_ctr++;
1369 }
1370 }
1371
1372 // If we have reached a significant number of updates and we haven't
1373 // accumulated too many cuts, then update bucket splits.
1374 const int CHANGE_THRESHOLD = nodes.size() / 6;
1375 if (update_ctr >= CHANGE_THRESHOLD && cut_storage->size() < 40) {
1376 // Clear union caches.
1377 fw_union_cache.clear();
1378 bw_union_cache.clear();
1379 print_info("Updating bucket splits with {} changes\n", update_ctr);
1380
1381 // Redefine buckets in parallel for both forward and backward
1382 // directions.
1383 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1384 work, bi_sched,
1385 SECTION {
1386 // Forward bucket redefinition.
1387 define_buckets<Direction::Forward>();
1388 },
1389 SECTION {
1390 // Backward bucket redefinition.
1391 define_buckets<Direction::Backward>();
1392 });
1393
1394 // Regenerate arcs after updating bucket splits.
1395 generate_arcs();
1396
1397 // Process jump bucket arcs in parallel.
1398 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1399 workB, bi_sched,
1400 SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>(); },
1401 SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>(); });
1402
1403 return true;
1404 }
1405 return false;
1406 }

◆ solve()

template<Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solve ( bool trigger = false)
inline

Solves the bucket graph optimization problem using a multi-stage bi-labeling algorithm.

The function iteratively adjusts the terminal time and handles different stages of the bi-labeling algorithm to find the optimal paths. The stages are adaptive and transition based on the inner objective values and iteration counts.

Definition at line 55 of file BucketSolve.h.

55 {
56 // Start with a nonoptimal status.
57 status = Status::NotOptimal;
58 if (trigger) {
59 transition = true;
60 fixed = false;
61 }
62
63 updateSplit(); // Update split values for the bucket graph.
64 std::vector<Label *> paths;
65
67 // Adaptive Stage Handling
69
70 // ----- Stage 1: Light Heuristic -----
71 if (s1 && depth == 0) {
72 stage = 1;
73 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::One>();
74 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
75 if (inner_obj >= -1 || iter >= 10) {
76 s1 = false;
77 s2 = true; // Transition to Stage 2.
78 }
79 }
80 // ----- Stage 2: Expensive Pricing Heuristic -----
81 else if (s2 && depth == 0) {
82 stage = 2;
83 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Two>();
84 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
85 if (inner_obj >= -10 || iter > 500) {
86 s2 = false;
87 s3 = true; // Transition directly to Stage 4.
88 }
89 }
90 // ----- Stage 3: Heuristic Fixing Approach -----
91 else if (s3 && depth == 0) {
92 stage = 3;
93 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Three>();
94 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
95 if (inner_obj >= -0.5) {
96 s3 = false;
97 s4 = true;
98 transition = true; // Prepare for Stage 4 transition.
99 }
100 }
101 // ----- Stage 4: Exact Labeling with Fixing Enabled -----
102 else if (s4 && depth == 0) {
103 stage = 4;
104#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
105 if (transition) {
106 // During a transition to Stage 4, fix buckets temporarily.
107 bool original_fixed = fixed;
108 fixed = true;
109 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>();
110 transition = false;
111 fixed = original_fixed;
112 min_red_cost = paths[0]->cost; // Update minimum reduced cost.
113 iter++;
114 return paths;
115 }
116#endif
117 // Standard Stage 4 processing.
118 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>();
119 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
120 bool rollback = false;
121 if (status != Status::Rollback) {
122 rollback = shallUpdateStep();
123 }
124 if (rollback) {
125 status = Status::Rollback;
126 return paths;
127 }
128 // Compute a new threshold for separation based on stats.
129 threshold = stats.computeThreshold(iter, inner_obj);
130 // If objective is sufficiently high, enter separation mode.
131 if (inner_obj > threshold) {
132 ss = true;
133#if !defined(SRC) && !defined(SRC3)
134 status = Status::Optimal;
135 return paths;
136#endif
137 status = Status::Separation;
138 }
139 }
140 // ----- Stage 5: Enumeration -----
141 else if (s5 && depth == 0) {
142 stage = 5;
143 print_info("Starting enumeration with gap {}\n", gap);
144 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Enumerate>();
145 print_info("Finished enumeration with {} paths\n", paths.size());
146 status = Status::Optimal;
147 }
148
149 // Increment the iteration counter.
150 iter++;
151 return paths;
152}

154 {

159
160 // Placeholder for the final paths (labels) and inner objective value
161 std::vector<Label *> paths;
162 double inner_obj;
163
165 // ADAPTIVE STAGE HANDLING
167 // Stage 1: Apply a light heuristic (Stage::One)
168 if (s1) {
169 stage = 1;
170 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::One>(); // Solve the problem with
171 // Stage 1 heuristic
172 inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
173
174 // Transition from Stage 1 to Stage 2 if the objective improves or after
175 // 10 iterations
176 if (inner_obj >= -5 || iter >= 10) {
177 s1 = false;
178 s2 = true; // Move to Stage 2
179 }
180 }
181 // Stage 2: Apply a more expensive pricing heuristic (Stage::Two)
182 else if (s2) {
183 s2 = true;
184 stage = 2;
185 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Two>(); // Solve the problem with
186 // Stage 2 heuristic
187 inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
188
189 // Transition from Stage 2 to Stage 3 if the objective improves or after
190 // 800 iterations
191 if (inner_obj >= -100 || iter > 800) {
192 status = Status::Optimal;
193 }
194 }
195
196 iter++; // Increment the iteration counter
197
198 return paths; // Return the final paths after processing
199}

◆ solvePSTEP()

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP ( PSTEPDuals & inner_pstep_duals)

Solves the PSTEP problem and returns a vector of labels representing paths.

This function performs the following steps:

  1. Resets the pool.
  2. Initializes the mono algorithm.
  3. Runs a labeling algorithm in the forward direction.
  4. Iterates through the forward buckets and computes new labels.
  5. Filters and collects labels that meet the criteria.
Returns
std::vector<Label*> A vector of pointers to Label objects representing the paths.

Definition at line 24 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

24 {
25
26 const int CHUNK_SIZE = 10;
27
28 std::vector<Label *> all_paths;
29 std::vector<double> all_costs;
30 std::vector<int> all_firsts, all_lasts;
31
32 // Mutex for shared resources
33 std::mutex cuts_mutex;
34
35 const int JOBS = nodes.size();
36 exec::static_thread_pool pool(std::thread::hardware_concurrency());
37 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
38
39 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> tasks;
40 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS - 1; ++i) {
41 for (int j = 1; j < JOBS; ++j) {
42 if (i == j) continue;
43 tasks.emplace_back(i, j);
44 }
45 }
46 auto bulk_sender =
47 stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + CHUNK_SIZE - 1) / CHUNK_SIZE, // Calculate total chunks
48 [&](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
49 // Calculate indices i, j from chunk_idx
50 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * CHUNK_SIZE;
51 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + CHUNK_SIZE, tasks.size());
52 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
53 auto [i, j] = tasks[task_idx];
54 BucketOptions sub_options;
55 auto sub_bg = BucketGraph(nodes, R_max[0], 20);
56 sub_bg.set_distance_matrix(distance_matrix);
57 sub_options.depot = i;
58 sub_options.end_depot = j;
59 // print i and j
60 sub_options.pstep = true; // print depot and end_depot
61 sub_options.max_path_size = options.max_path_size;
62 sub_options.min_path_size = options.min_path_size;
63 sub_bg.setOptions(sub_options);
64 sub_bg.setPSTEPduals(inner_pstep_duals);
65 sub_bg.setup();
66 sub_bg.reset_pool();
67 sub_bg.mono_initialization();
68 // Solve and get the paths
69 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(sub_bg.fw_buckets.size());
70 forward_cbar = sub_bg.labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, Stage::Four, Full::PSTEP>();
71 std::vector<Label *> paths;
72 // print paths.size
73 for (auto bucket : std::ranges::iota_view(0, sub_bg.fw_buckets_size)) {
74 auto bucket_labels = sub_bg.fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
75 // print bucket_labels size
76 for (auto label : bucket_labels) {
77 if (!label) continue;
78 auto new_label = compute_mono_label(label);
79 if ((new_label->nodes_covered.size() > 1) &&
80 (new_label->nodes_covered.back() == sub_options.end_depot) &&
81 (new_label->nodes_covered.size() >= options.min_path_size &&
82 new_label->nodes_covered.size() <= options.max_path_size)) {
83 paths.push_back(new_label);
84 }
85 }
86 }
87
88 // Check if no paths are found
89 if (paths.empty()) return;
90
91 // Lock to protect shared resources
92 {
93 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(cuts_mutex);
94 // Process the found paths
95 for (const auto &path : paths) { all_paths.push_back(path); }
96 }
97 // }
98 }
99 });
100
101 // Submit work to the thread pool and wait for completion
102 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
103 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
104
105 return all_paths;
106}
